description: This spell enables them to share more completely with the forces of lightning allowing them their hand eye coordination to reach extraordinary levels. This in effectively will give them +8 DFT for the duration of the spell.
short_description: This spell enables them to share more completely with the forces of lightning allowing them their hand eye coordination to reach extraordinary levels.

description: When cast upon any metal headed or bladed weapon (not missile weapons), the weapon will gain +2 lightning damage each round, for up to 6 rounds. If the weapon hits on any round then all pluses that it has gained will be discharged upon the target and the weapon will begin to charge again from +2. Thus if the weapon did not hit until 6R after the spell was cast, it could cause +12 damage. Such damage ignores armour (apart from full plate), and will also discharge down a metal parrying weapon like a sword. Once hit by such a weapon the target must make a stun roll. If the weapon does not hit after 6R the charge it has will remain at its current level for a further 1R after which it will simply discharge to the ground.
short_description: When cast upon any metal headed or bladed weapon (not missile weapons), the weapon will gain +2 lightning damage each round, for up to 6 rounds.

description: Allows the caster to shoot a lightning bolt from their casting hand once every two rounds whilst the spell is in effect. The bolt has a range of 3Sd and an accuracy of 50%. Even if it misses the intended target there is still a 50% chance that it will hit anyone stood 1Sd either side, otherwise it simply strikes the ground. The bolt will cause 1D10 damage +1 for every level of the caster, the target must also roll for a stun. The bolt will ignore most armour and cause damage somewhere on the target’s body although full plate will provide normal protection. NB the bolt can also be used to ignite reasonably flammable material like oil or hay.
short_description: Allows the caster to shoot a lightning bolt from their casting hand once every two rounds whilst the spell is in effect. The bolt has a range of 3Sd and an accuracy of 50%.

description: Allows the caster to set a Sarr glyph upon an object. Once placed the spell will remain dormant until a human sized creature comes within 4Sd of the object. Once this occurs tiny sparks will begin to arc across the object’s surface charging up the glyph. This process will take 1D4 rounds, after which the glyph will discharge a single bolt of lightning at a random creature in the 4Sd range (the caster excepted), The bolt will cause 1D20 points of damage and will, ignore all but plate armour the target must roll for a stun.
short_description: Allows the caster to set a Sarr glyph upon an object. Once placed the spell will remain dormant until a human sized creature comes within 4Sd of the object.

description: Creates a crackling shield of energy just in front of the caster. The shield will not stop a physical attack from a weapon but will cause a shock to anyone attempting to hit the caster through it. The shock will travel down any handheld or natural weapons and cause 1D6 damage that ignores armour as well as a stun effect for the remainder of the round.
short_description: Creates a crackling shield of energy just in front of the caster. The shield will not stop a physical attack from a weapon but will cause a shock to anyone attempting to hit the caster through it.

description: Generates a glowing blue sphere about any chosen point within range. The sphere will have a diameter of 1Sd per level of the caster. Alternatively the spell can be set to operate as a barrier between any two parallel objects with a gap and height up to 2Sd per level of the caster. Anyone passing in or out of the sphere or barrier will receive 1D4 electrical damage for each level of the caster and must also roll for a stun. The damage ignores armour (except full plate). Anyone within the sphere will be unaffected unless they attempt to leave.
short_description: Generates a glowing blue sphere about any chosen point within range. The sphere will have a diameter of 1Sd per level of the caster.

description: Offers the recipient a triggered defence against lighting for the duration of the spell. Should the target be struck by damaging lightning, magical or natural, the spell will be triggered, giving the target complete immunity from being struck by lightning for 6R. Once the spell has been triggered it will need to be re-cast to gain further protection.
short_description: Offers the recipient a triggered defence against lighting for the duration of the spell.

description: Will create an illusion of dancing shafts of lightning. The caster may create the lightning anywhere in range from just the size of a spark up to an area up to 4Sd across. The lightning is are merely illusory, will give off no heat and will not burn or shock anything it touches. The lightning will, however provide a flickering illumination in keeping with the size of the illusion.
short_description: Will create an illusion of dancing shafts of lightning. The caster may create the lightning anywhere in range from just the size of a spark up to an area up to 4Sd across.

description: Enables the caster to magnetise a large iron or steel item. The bigger the item the better. Once magnetised it will attract any smaller iron or steel items to it with a strength of 20 at 1Sd dropping by 2 for every further Sd from the magnetised item. Thus the item would attract with a STR of 10 at 5Sd distance. The caster and their accoutrements may also be affected.
short_description: Enables the caster to magnetise a large iron or steel item. The bigger the item the better.

description: Summons a lesser lightning spirit. The spirit will manifest as a glowing ball of energy occasionally giving off long flickering sparks. Such a spirit may be directed to provide illumination up to 3Sd or to hinder the progress of a creature by blocking its path, should anyone come within 1Sd of the ball they have an 80% chance of being struck by one of the discharges whereupon they must roll for a stun.
short_description: Summons a lesser lightning spirit. The spirit will manifest as a glowing ball of energy occasionally giving off long flickering sparks.

description: Wards the caster from direct attacks by Sarr magic and Lightning spirits. The spell will give complete protection from any 3 glyph Sarr spells and attacks by manifest lesser Lightning elementals for the duration of the spell. It will protect from 4 glyph Sarr spells and attacks by greater Lightning elementals for 1R before being dispelled. It will be dispelled without affecting any 5 glyph spells or the attack of an Lightning drake.
short_description: Wards the caster from direct attacks by Sarr magic and Lightning spirits.

description: Creates a compass that will point only in the direction a particular person, object or place. The spell requires an iron needle and a small sample of material from the person, object or place such as hair, a splinter of wood or a chip of stone. When cast the material and the needle are fused together on the caster’s palm in a flash of bright light. Once the spell ends, all the material on the needle will have been consumed and more will be needed if the spell is to be re-cast.
short_description: Creates a compass that will point only in the direction a particular person, object or place.

description: Enables the caster to listen and talk to the spirits of lightning. To do so requires the caster to vigorously rub a piece of amber against some fleece or soft animal pelt. Holding the amber to the ear allows the caster to hear the crackling and buzzing speech of the lighting elementals. Most lighting elementals are very talkative and will likely chat about absolute rubbish for the duration of the spell. If the caster can interrupt long enough to ask a question, the elemental may reply at length, though the information should never be trusted entirely as truthful.
short_description: Enables the caster to listen and talk to the spirits of lightning. To do so requires the caster to vigorously rub a piece of amber against some fleece or soft animal pelt.

description: If the caster holds a metal rod or plate (or other metal implement no larger than 1Sd in length, like a sword) and touches it to another piece of metal this spell allows the caster to channel a spark of lightning from their finger through the metal and into the other. Where the two pieces of metal are making contact they will weld together at that point. This may be repeated once each round whilst the spell is in effect. When the spell ends such welds will remain strong and permanent.
short_description: If the caster holds a metal rod or plate (or other metal implement no larger than 1Sd in length, like a sword) and touches it to another piece of metal this spell allows the caster to channel a spa...

description: Wraps the caster in a shimmering cloak of lightning. The cloak will illuminate up to 5Sd with flickering blue/white light. The cloak is confusing for others to look at directly, reducing an opponents attacks by 20%. Additionally should the caster be struck by a weapon the cloak will discharge a shock through the attackers weapon. They must roll for stun and also if the weapon is metal the attacker must make a DFTx3 to avoid dropping it.
short_description: Wraps the caster in a shimmering cloak of lightning. The cloak will illuminate up to 5Sd with flickering blue/white light.

description: Enables the caster to turn a humanoid target, and their accoutrements, to an incandescent energy form. The caster may use the spell on themselves if desired. The recipient will appear as an arcing and flickering outline and skeletal form. The recipient will be able to writhe in this form but cannot move far from the spot where the spell was cast. If the recipient was already moving when the spell was cast, like riding a horse or falling, they will stop moving and float were they are. The recipient will illuminate an area of 3Sd radius about them. In this form the recipient may see and hear, but will be unable to talk. They will also be unable to cast spells involving other elements, and other spells which might normally affect the body or accoutrements (for example healing) may fail as the spirits may not understand the relevance. If the recipient is struck by a weapon it will pass harmlessly through them, although if the weapon is mostly metal the wielder will receive a shock and must roll DFTx3 or drop the weapon. Other objects placed within them will be flung out before the spell ends. The recipient will take no damage from falling, fire, cold, acid, or drowning. They may be affected by magic depending upon its effect.
short_description: Enables the caster to turn a humanoid target, and their accoutrements, to an incandescent energy form. The caster may use the spell on themselves if desired.

description: This removes all pain from the subject’s body. The spell will slowly dull the ability to feel pain until the recipient can feel nothing but the pressure of contact on their body. The spell will also heal chronic pain from conditions such as backache or long term illness. Sometimes offering relief for days even weeks. Once cast the recipient’s skin will coruscate with tiny sparks and their hair will stand on end. As a useful benefit this will also enable the recipient to remain conscious and able to move even when their Hit Points are at 0 or lower. In theory they would be able to continue functioning on negative HP whilst they are dying and their VGR is being depleted at whatever rate their wounds have determined. Once their VGR is at 0 they will drop dead, but at least it will have been painless.
short_description: This removes all pain from the subject’s body. The spell will slowly dull the ability to feel pain until the recipient can feel nothing but the pressure of contact on their body.

description: Enables the caster to turn an animal target, and its accoutrements, to an incandescent energy form. The beast will appear as an arcing and flickering outline and skeletal form. The beast will be able to writhe in this form but cannot move far from the spot where the spell was cast. If the beast was already moving when the spell was cast, like running or falling, they will stop moving and float were they are. The beast will illuminate an area of 3Sd radius about it. In this form the beast may see and hear, but will be unable to make sounds. If the beast is struck by a weapon it will pass harmlessly through it, although if the weapon is mostly metal the wielder will receive a shock and must roll DFTx3 or drop the weapon. Other objects placed within it will be flung out before the spell ends. The beast will take no damage from falling, fire, cold, acid, or drowning. It may be affected by magic depending upon its effect.
short_description: Enables the caster to turn an animal target, and its accoutrements, to an incandescent energy form. The beast will appear as an arcing and flickering outline and skeletal form.

description: Turns an object the size of shield or small pack to into a to an incandescent energy form (up to the size of a large chest for level 5) .The object will illuminate an area of roughly 2Sd radius but may be seen from some considerable distance. If the object is struck by a weapon it will pass harmlessly through it, although if the weapon is mostly metal the wielder will receive a shock and must roll DFTx3 or drop the weapon.. The object will also take no damage from falling, fire, cold, acid, water or lightning. The object cannot be moved whilst in this form. Another object placed within it will be flung out before the spell ends.
short_description: Turns an object the size of shield or small pack to into a to an incandescent energy form (up to the size of a large chest for level 5) .The object will illuminate an area of roughly 2Sd radius but...

description: Releases a glowing ball of energy from the caster’s hand. The ball will mostly travel in straight lines hovering up to 1Sd above the ground. At times it will change direction to follow a contour or avoid a solid obstruction. However the ball can pass straight through the walls of less substantial buildings or the hulls of ships. The ball will move at roughly 20Sd each round and will continue travelling for the entire duration of the spell. Anyone within 2Sd of where it passes suffer 1D4 heat damage. Anyone within 1Sd will suffer 1D10 damage and must make a keyx2 luck roll or suffer a shock and subsequent stun. Anyone unfortunate to actually come into contact with the ball will suffer 2D10 damage and a shock that and stun. They must also make a VGR/CONx2 roll or go into respiratory arrest. Due to the less predictable nature of its movement the chances of actually hitting a specific target (even a large one) will only be equivalent to the caster’s key.
short_description: Releases a glowing ball of energy from the caster’s hand. The ball will mostly travel in straight lines hovering up to 1Sd above the ground.

description: Will spread a high, roiling cloud of smoke, extending to the limits of the spell. Soon after, random bolts of lightning will begin to strike anywhere within range of the spell. Such bolts will set fire to anything readily flammable. The chance of being struck by a lightning bolt is 15% for each round that someone remains in the area of the spell. Since a strike is fairly instantaneous, there is almost no chance to dodge, although full plate armour will offer complete protection. If struck by a lightning bolt a target will suffer 1D10+4 damage. The strikes are indiscriminate of friend or foe and only the caster will be immune.
short_description: Will spread a high, roiling cloud of smoke, extending to the limits of the spell. Soon after, random bolts of lightning will begin to strike anywhere within range of the spell.

description: Generates an incandescent arcing blue sphere about any chosen point within range. The sphere will have a diameter of 2Sd per level of the caster. Alternatively the spell can be set to operate as a barrier between any two parallel objects with a gap and height up to 4Sd and a thickness of 2Lk per level of the caster. Anyone passing in or out of the sphere or barrier will receive 1D4+1 electrical damage for each level of the caster and must also roll for a stun. The damage ignores armour (full plate reduces damage by a half). Anyone within the sphere will be unaffected unless they attempt to pass out of it. Anyone remaining within the barrier will be subject to damage each round.
short_description: Generates an incandescent arcing blue sphere about any chosen point within range. The sphere will have a diameter of 2Sd per level of the caster.

description: Dismisses a lesser lightning spirit. If cast at a greater or high lightning spirit it will cause it to stop its actions for 1R.
short_description: Dismisses a lesser lightning spirit. If cast at a greater or high lightning spirit it will cause it to stop its actions for 1R.

description: Wards an area of 20Sd radius about the caster from direct attacks by Sarr magic and Lightning spirits. The spell will give complete protection from any 4 glyph (or fewer) Sarr spells and attacks by manifest greater Lightning elementals for the duration of the spell. It will protect from 5 glyph Sarr spells and attacks by Lightning drakes for 1R before being dispelled. If the caster moves, the area protected will move with them.
short_description: Wards an area of 20Sd radius about the caster from direct attacks by Sarr magic and Lightning spirits.

description: Summons a Greater Lightning Spirit and enables it to manifest. Such a spirit may be asked to attack a target, but may only do so for 6R before it must return to its element. It may also be asked to bear a rider for the duration of the spell.
short_description: Summons a Greater Lightning Spirit and enables it to manifest. Such a spirit may be asked to attack a target, but may only do so for 6R before it must return to its element.

description: Enables the caster (and their accoutrements) to turn to an incandescent energy form and allows them to move about like a spirit. The caster will appear as a flickering concentration of lightning energy that will illuminate an area of 4Sd radius about it. They may move unhindered in any direction through air, metal or water at twice their normal move. In this form the caster may see and hear but will not be able to talk. They will also be unable to cast spells involving other elements, and other spells which might normally affect the body or accoutrements (for example healing) may fail as the spirits may not understand the relevance. If the caster is struck by a weapon it will pass harmlessly through them, although if the weapon is mostly metal the wielder will receive a shock and must roll DFTx3 or drop the weapon. The caster will take no damage from falling, fire, cold, acid, drowning or lightning. They may be affected by magic depending upon its effect. They may also pass through a metal conduit (like a wire) instantaneously but must make a KEYx4 roll to reform at the other end. If they fail this roll they will loose their form. They may try again on successive rounds to regain their form but cannot undertake any other actions. If the caster is still not whole by the end of the spell they will lose cohesion entirely and their soul will be lost amongst the spirits.
short_description: Enables the caster (and their accoutrements) to turn to an incandescent energy form and allows them to move about like a spirit.

description: Causes the recipient to become a sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd radius about them from direct attacks by Lightning magic and Lightning spirits. The sentinel will give complete protection from any 5 symbol SARR spells and attacks by manifest Lightning drakes for the duration of the spell. If the sentinel is killed then the spell will cease to function. The sentinel will appear to be crackle with energy for the entire duration of the spell, their hair will stand on end and sparks will jump from them to anything they come into contact with. Having their body and minds imbued with such power for such a period of time has its cost. Whilst the sentinel will feel no ill effects when the spell is active, they will fall twitching to the ground as soon as it ends. The sentinel’s INT will temporarily be reduced to 1 which will recover at a point an hour (consciousness will return after 2-3). Any memories of the day will be permanently erased, as will some crucial memories of family or friends.
short_description: Causes the recipient to become a sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd radius about them from direct attacks by Lightning magic and Lightning spirits.

description: Causes the target object to become a sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd radius about it from direct attacks by Lightning magic and Lightning spirits. The sentinel will give complete protection from any 5 symbol SARR spells and attacks by manifest Lightning drakes for the duration of the spell. Statues or carved stones can often be found on the walls of ancient buildings or cities to be used for this purpose. The sentinel object will appear to be crackle with energy for the entire duration of the spell.
short_description: Causes the target object to become a sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd radius about it from direct attacks by Lightning magic and Lightning spirits.

description: Dismisses a lesser or greater lightning spirit. If cast at a high lightning spirit it will cause it to stop its actions for 1R..
short_description: Dismisses a lesser or greater lightning spirit. If cast at a high lightning spirit it will cause it to stop its actions for 1R..

description: Summons a High Lightning Spirit and allows it to manifest at any point within range of the spell. The spirit will take the form of a long sinuous glowing blue/white dragon which will illuminate an area of 10Sd radius with its flickering light. The dragon will obey the caster attacking foes or buildings etc. as directed
short_description: Summons a High Lightning Spirit and allows it to manifest at any point within range of the spell.

description: Spreads a high, roiling cloud in a ring above the caster. Soon after, random bolts of lightning will begin to strike anywhere within range of the spell. Initially the ring will be in a 40Sd radius circle above of the point of casting. But the caster may push the width of the whole ring outwards creating an unaffected area in the centre. Doing this will reduce the width of the band of affected ground at the same time. Thus if the ring is pushed out to a 50Sd radius, a 20Sd radius unaffected area could be made in the centre but the width of the affected band would have been reduced to just 30Sd. The lightning bolts can set fire to anything readily flammable that is struck. The chance of anything in range being struck by a lightning bolt is 15% each round. Since a strike is fairly instantaneous, there is almost no chance to dodge, although full plate armour will offer complete protection. If struck by a lightning bolt a target will suffer 1D10+4 damage . The strikes are indiscriminate of friend or foe and only the caster will be immune.
short_description: Spreads a high, roiling cloud in a ring above the caster. Soon after, random bolts of lightning will begin to strike anywhere within range of the spell.

description: Dismisses a manifest lesser, greater or high Lightning elemental spirit.
short_description: Dismisses a manifest lesser, greater or high Lightning elemental spirit.
Spell Glyphs: Sarr + Maef + Ghan + Ovet + Eyama + Chom

description: Causes one flowering plant to bud and bloom. If it is already flowering it will produce more. If used on a leafless, but living plant it will cause it to produce leaves over the course of the spell. Used on a cutting, it will take root where placed.
short_description: Causes one flowering plant to bud and bloom. If it is already flowering it will produce more.

description: The plant on which this spell is used will become sickly and withered, dropping leaves as if in autumn, whilst flowers or fruit will shrivel and fall. The spell will not kill a large or healthy plant and, given reasonable conditions, it will show signs of recovery within a few days.
short_description: The plant on which this spell is used will become sickly and withered, dropping leaves as if in autumn, whilst flowers or fruit will shrivel and fall.

description: Allows the caster to train plants and bushes upon which this spell is cast, sticks or stalks may be bent and will remain in position.
short_description: Allows the caster to train plants and bushes upon which this spell is cast, sticks or stalks may be bent and will remain in position.

description: Makes the caster more resistant to mundane plant damage. Their clothing or skin will be less likely to become snagged by thorns or burs. They are less likely to marked by stains from the juice of plants. The spell will also offer a +10% chance of avoiding being stung by a poisonous plant.
short_description: Makes the caster more resistant to mundane plant damage. Their clothing or skin will be less likely to become snagged by thorns or burs.

description: If the caster plants a seed or root or touches a plant this spell will cause it to sprout and grow to the equivalent of a year’s growth. If cast generally it will sprout a random plant of an indigenous variety somewhere in range. Whilst the spell is working the plant will remain healthy. When the spell ends the plant will continue to grow or perish as normal in the given conditions.
short_description: If the caster plants a seed or root or touches a plant this spell will cause it to sprout and grow to the equivalent of a year’s growth.

description: Enables an enchanter to detect the presence of a specific plant type within range of the spell. The caster must hold an image of the plant in their mind at the time of casting, and may need to make a plant lore roll if the plant is not familiar to them. Even if the plant is dormant at the time of casting, the spell can still lead the caster to tubers or roots in the ground. Ranges are as follows: level 1 20Sd level 2 40Sd level 3 60Sd level 4 80Sd level 5 100Sd
short_description: Enables an enchanter to detect the presence of a specific plant type within range of the spell.

description: Affects a wooden weapon such as a club or staff, or wooden hafted weapon like a mace or spear. Up to six arrows can be affected at once. It will cause the wood near the business end to sprout a number of green tendrils and thorns. If the weapon overcomes an opponent’s armour protection when it hits, they will also suffer 1D4 points of VGR damage caused by a stinging sap getting into the wound. The VGR damage is recovered at the normal rate. Once the spell is finished the tendrils will die back and fall away.
short_description: Affects a wooden weapon such as a club or staff, or wooden hafted weapon like a mace or spear. Up to six arrows can be affected at once.

description: Causes small faintly luminous fungi to grow in a ring up to 2Sd radius about the caster. Anyone or anything big enough to be a threat attempting to walk through, or step over, the ring will cause the fungi at that point to emit a cloud of irritating spores. The invader must attempt to roll under their VGR or begin coughing and sneezing for 6R. This will also reduce any attacks by 20.
short_description: Causes small faintly luminous fungi to grow in a ring up to 2Sd radius about the caster.

description: Enables the caster to heal 1HP per level on any wound or damage that a plant may have suffered. A plant lore roll should first be made if the plant is to be restored properly otherwise the plant may grow abnormally or become diseased as a result. NB if the caster uses Key from Arbine then 2 points per level may be restored.
short_description: Enables the caster to heal 1HP per level on any wound or damage that a plant may have suffered.

description: Simply touching a plant after this spell is cast will cause it 1 Hit Point of damage per level of the caster. The spell will cause the affected area of the plant to wither and rot. This can easily kill small plants. In general terms, small plants and bushes only have an effective KEY of 1 to overcome, whilst some older trees and unusual species of sentient plants might have a KEY attribute that is equivalent to a person.
short_description: Simply touching a plant after this spell is cast will cause it 1 Hit Point of damage per level of the caster. The spell will cause the affected area of the plant to wither and rot.

description: This combination will allow the caster to train even large plants upon which this spell is cast, limbs or branches may be bent and will remain in position. Using this spell does not make things easier to bend it will still require the same force although the limb will not break or spring bask as a result. Thus even a tree trunk may be bent although it would require considerable force ( rope, pulleys, leverage) in order to bend it.
short_description: This combination will allow the caster to train even large plants upon which this spell is cast, limbs or branches may be bent and will remain in position.

description: Affects a wooden weapon such as a club or staff, or wooden hafted weapon like a mace, spear. It can affect up to six arrows the caster touches whilst the spell is in effect. The spell will cause the wood near the business end of the weapon to sprout a profusion of green tendrils and thorns. The thorns will cause the weapon to do +1 damage on a successful hit. Additionally, if the weapon overcomes an opponent’s armour protection when it hits, they will also suffer 1 point of VGR/CON damage per level of the caster caused by a poisonous sap entering the wound. Thus a level 4 enchanter could wield a simple staff that can cause 4 points of poison damage. This VGR/CON damage is recovered at the normal rate. Once the spell is finished the tendrils will die back and fall away.
short_description: Affects a wooden weapon such as a club or staff, or wooden hafted weapon like a mace, spear. It can affect up to six arrows the caster touches whilst the spell is in effect.

description: Causes the affected plant to produce a fruit, tumour or other woody growth over the course of 2R. The growth will be about the size of a man’s fist. If the spell is used indoors or below ground a fungal growth will appear. Should anyone else come within 1Sd of the plant there is a 70% chance it will explode releasing a hail of poison dust or spores into the air. The victim should make a luck roll (Key x2) to avoid breathing the spores or it getting in their eye. The poison will usually be a type 5 poison causing paralysis of the victim. The poison will have a strength of 20 which will attack the DFT each round after exposure. Each time the poison successfully attacks the victim will loose a point from their STR AG and DFT. This will continue until each attribute is reduced to 1. Once completely paralysed the victim will remain in that state for 40-VGR minutes after which they will begin to recover their attributes at a rate of 1 point each minute. This spell may be cast upon any tree or plant (except Meris trees, and Witchblooms).
short_description: Causes the affected plant to produce a fruit, tumour or other woody growth over the course of 2R. The growth will be about the size of a man’s fist.

description: Causes any large plant, or group of smaller plants to grow thorny tendrils and attack anyone who walks close. The attacks consist of 4 grabbing and scratching attacks each round. Each tendril will have 5+1D4 HP, and a STR of 2D4+4. They may make two grab and two scratch attacks each round, as follows: Weapon attack damage parry Grab 50% * Scratch 50% 1D4+3 *The grab attack will do no damage, but will hold the victim fast, unless they break free in a STR vs. STR struggle or the tendril is cut. The victim should make a luck roll to determine whether they have an arm free when grabbed.
short_description: Causes any large plant, or group of smaller plants to grow thorny tendrils and attack anyone who walks close. The attacks consist of 4 grabbing and scratching attacks each round.

description: Renders the recipient immune to the effects of plant toxins, stings or ingested poisons for the duration of the spell. It will not cure someone already suffering from any of these effects, but provided it is cast before any damage is taken from such a toxin its preventative effects may be able to act in time.
short_description: Renders the recipient immune to the effects of plant toxins, stings or ingested poisons for the duration of the spell.

description: Causes a 2Sd diameter briar patch to grow over an area the caster indicates within range over course of 2R. The plants will form long thorny tendrils up to 2Sd long. Such a patch can be summoned on a patch of ground, flagstones or even flooring anywhere but places the sun never shines. Anyone attempting to pass through the patch will become snagged by 1D4 thorny tendrils. Each tendril has 5STR and 5HP and will cause 1 point of damage each round that the person is moving. Once entangled a person cannot leave the patch until they cut, break or disentangle the tendrils. Each round that they are moving in the patch they risk becoming entangled by 1D4 more tendrils. Should an entangled person stop moving they will not become any further entangled and will not take any damage. The patch will take damage from fire, but a torch will be insufficient to out-damage the rate of re-growth. Magical fire or flames fed by an accelerant such as oil will cause enough damage each round to take hold. Once the spell ends the plants will become dead and brittle and may be broken through without difficulty.
short_description: Causes a 2Sd diameter briar patch to grow over an area the caster indicates within range over course of 2R. The plants will form long thorny tendrils up to 2Sd long.

description: Will conceal plant or group of similar plants up to the size of a large tree by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle. This effectively gives the plant a 100% hide, especially if viewed from one angle from some distance. The illusion will remain completely convincing until either the viewer or the plant moves, when the camouflage briefly ceases to match what is behind and must re-orient itself on the following round. If there is more than one viewer the illusion will struggle to create multiple background colours that will begin to overlap to meet each separate viewpoint rendering the concealment less effective.
short_description: Will conceal plant or group of similar plants up to the size of a large tree by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle.

description: Enables the caster to create the illusion of vegetation over an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions. The illusion will be a generic wall of plants/bushes/vines/trees covering the area that would be appropriate to the local flora. Alternatively the illusion may be a duplication of another vegetation within range of the spell as indicated by the caster during casting. The illusion will be convincingly three dimensional until walked through.
short_description: Enables the caster to create the illusion of vegetation over an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions.

description: This will hide the caster amongst any kind of vegetation, covering them with colours, smells and leafy growths to match whatever vegetation is growing around them. When still the caster will be almost unnoticeable only visible if someone looking makes a critical see roll. When moving the spell is less effective but the caster will still only be seen at half see chance.
short_description: This will hide the caster amongst any kind of vegetation, covering them with colours, smells and leafy growths to match whatever vegetation is growing around them.

description: Causes a group of plant stems to grow over the course of several minutes in the area the caster touches. The plant will twist and twine together into a roughly humanoid shape. Once grown the plant will remain alive as long as it receives water and nutrients as any plant would. But will die off in winter leaving a dried human shape of stems.
short_description: Causes a group of plant stems to grow over the course of several minutes in the area the caster touches. The plant will twist and twine together into a roughly humanoid shape.

description: Causes any vegetables, plant based food to spoil, become blighted or grow mouldy, becoming inedible. The effect may take up to 12R to become apparent depending on the area affected. This can include a crop in a field up to the range of the spell.
short_description: Causes any vegetables, plant based food to spoil, become blighted or grow mouldy, becoming inedible. The effect may take up to 12R to become apparent depending on the area affected.

description: Causes various plants within range of the spell germinate grow and yield enough edible fruit, vegetables, leaves and roots to feed two people for one day. The food produced must be found and collected and a plant lore roll may be necessary to avoid picking any inedible fruit etc. which may have been in the area before the spell was cast. Using this spell in the dead of winter can be effective, however using it in a desert, or icy waste where no plants ever grow will likely result in failure.
short_description: Causes various plants within range of the spell germinate grow and yield enough edible fruit, vegetables, leaves and roots to feed two people for one day.

description: Summons a lesser plant spirit. Such a spirit will rarely actually manifest but are said to appear as a tiny impish humanoid figure. Such an spirit can be asked to scout for the caster and, though mute, can mime what they have seen quite effectively. Lesser plant spirits may also be asked to attack an opponent or hinder their passage in some way. The spirit may cause undergrowth to wrap itself round people’s or horses’ legs, overhanging boughs may droop even lower and nuts or fruit or rotten branches might fall from trees. They are also believed to be able to warp arrow shafts and crack the odd spoke on a cart wheel as well as cause really-hard-to-remove stains to appear on people’s clothing.
short_description: Summons a lesser plant spirit. Such a spirit will rarely actually manifest but are said to appear as a tiny impish humanoid figure.

description: Protects the recipient plant from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at it up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 glyph spells will be repelled without affecting this spell. 3 glyph spells or the attacks of lesser elementals will only be warded for 1R before this spell fails. NB this counter spell is only really effective against spells which are aimed at the protected plant, spells which cause harm through a secondary physical effect such as creating an ice cloud may not be cancelled.
short_description: Protects the recipient plant from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at it up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 glyph spells will be repelled without affecting this spell.

description: Enables the caster to make fine leafy green tendrils grow and multiply wherever they gesture within range. The garlands will swell, bud and flower where the caster wishes filling the air with a sweet aroma. Whilst they are not particularly strong individually such tendrils could be wrapped around and around someone or something to help secure them. Multiplied the tendrils would have the equivalent strength of rope.
short_description: Enables the caster to make fine leafy green tendrils grow and multiply wherever they gesture within range.

description: Acts to make plants move their boughs out of the way of the caster like curtains parting for dramatic effect. It may also be used to stop timber or piles of potatoes etc. falling.
short_description: Acts to make plants move their boughs out of the way of the caster like curtains parting for dramatic effect. It may also be used to stop timber or piles of potatoes etc.

description: Restores 2 Hit Points per level of the caster to a damaged plant. This may even be effective if part of the plant has been broken off provided the broken piece is held in place while the spell is cast. If this spell is cast using Key stored in Arbine the effect may be doubled.
short_description: Restores 2 Hit Points per level of the caster to a damaged plant.

description: Causes a small plant or part of a larger plant up to the size of a small bush to blacken and crumble to dust. The attack will cause 1D4 HP damage, +1 HP per level to a larger plant. This might include a section of the trunk of a tree weakening it and, with sufficient damage, causing it to fall. It my also break or damage a timber in a building, an axle on a cart, or a tiller on a boat. Plant spirits are also vulnerable to this spell.
short_description: Causes a small plant or part of a larger plant up to the size of a small bush to blacken and crumble to dust. The attack will cause 1D4 HP damage, +1 HP per level to a larger plant.

description: Causes plants to grow up round the recipient’s body over the course of 2R. The plants will become woody and hard providing a tough flexible layer of armour, often still covered in green leaves. Any damage done to the armour will grow back whilst the spell is in effect. The armour will provide 1D6+4 points of protection all over the recipient’s body and will only reduce their AG by 2. Whilst the armour is growing the recipient must remain in the same spot. Once the growing process is finished the roots will detach from the ground as the recipient lifts their leg, and re-attach when they put it down. Should the recipient suffer a knockback attack they will be able to resist at +12 on their roll due to the stability provided by the roots. The greenery will also provide excellent camouflage and the recipient’s hide skill will be increased by +40% in woodland or vegetation. Once the spell ends the plants will become dead and brittle and may easily by pulled off.
short_description: Causes plants to grow up round the recipient’s body over the course of 2R. The plants will become woody and hard providing a tough flexible layer of armour, often still covered in green leaves.

description: Turns a humanoid target, and their accoutrements, into a tree, causing them to take root on the spot and growing twigs and leaves. The caster may use the spell on themselves if desired. The recipient will maintain roughly their normal form, but elongated and twisted, their arms and hair forming branches and twigs. Whilst in this form they will not be able to move other than rustling the odd leaf. They will be unable to see. and cannot cast magic. Although they will be able to hear and feel hot or cold. They will feel mild pain if struck but will take half damage from cutting or impaling weapons whilst the spell is in effect. The recipient will have an effective skin armour of 5 points and a blow will need to exceed this to do any damage. NB a critical simply means it does double damage, the critical tables cannot be used to ignore armour. In this form the recipient will be resistant to high winds, drowning, poison gas and plant based poisons. They will take normal damage from fire,cold or lightning.
short_description: Turns a humanoid target, and their accoutrements, into a tree, causing them to take root on the spot and growing twigs and leaves. The caster may use the spell on themselves if desired.

description: An unusual spell that is not always appreciated by the recipient. But then, in the field, desperate situations can call for desperate solutions. The spell will only work on a recipient who has lost a limb or part of a limb. To work, the caster must thrust a suitable seed from a large woody plant like a bush or tree into the stump. A hole may need to be cut into the stump to facilitate this. Once the spell is cast the seed will begin to sprout leaves and grow multiple intertwined stalks from the end of the stump roughly forming the shape of the recipient’s lost limb. At the same time the plant will send roots deep into the recipient merging the plant and limb as one. This process will take the entire duration of the spell and will cause the recipient to suffer intense pain. Once the plant has grown, the stalks will become hard and woody, though they may retain their leaves for days or months. Whilst the wooden limb that grows will be a rough facsimile of the original it will not be jointed or have any form of movement –it will simply be a wooden limb. The recipient may also find that the pain from the plant’s roots, or their lost limb, may never fully subside.
short_description: An unusual spell that is not always appreciated by the recipient. But then, in the field, desperate situations can call for desperate solutions.

description: Enables the caster to turn an animal target, and its accoutrements, into a tree, causing it to take root on the spot and growing twigs and leaves. The beast will become elongated and twisted, its limbs forming branches and twigs. Whilst in this form it will not be able to move other than rustling the odd leaf. It will be unable to see and cannot make sounds. Although it will be able to hear and feel hot or cold. It will feel mild pain if struck but will take half damage from bludgeoning or impaling weapons whilst the spell is in effect. The beast will have an effective skin armour of 10 points and a blow will need to exceed this to do any damage. NB a critical simply means it does double damage, the critical tables cannot be used to ignore armour. In this form the beast will be resistant to high winds, drowning, poison gas and plant based poisons. It will take normal damage from fire,cold or lightning.
short_description: Enables the caster to turn an animal target, and its accoutrements, into a tree, causing it to take root on the spot and growing twigs and leaves.

description: Protects the target object and its contents from the effects of damage from living and growing plants. This can include noxious or corrosive powders and sprays given off by plants. The spell will also protect from the physical results of attacks by plant elementals or plant spells that would otherwise cause damage. The spell will be effective on objects such as a large building or ship that would fit within the radius of the spell’s area of effect.
short_description: Protects the target object and its contents from the effects of damage from living and growing plants. This can include noxious or corrosive powders and sprays given off by plants.

description: This spell will affect random plants within range of the spell to grow thorny tendrils and attack anyone who walks close to them. The chance of anyone in the area being close enough to an affected plant will be 20% each round The attacks consist of 4 grabbing and scratching attacks each round. Each tendril will have 5+1D4 HP, and a STR of 2D4+4. They may make two grab and two scratch attacks each round, as follows: Weapon attack damage parry Grab 50% * Scratch 50% 1D4+3 *The grab attack will do no damage, but will hold the victim fast, unless they break free in a STR vs. STR struggle or the tendril is cut. The victim should make a luck roll to determine whether they have an arm free when grabbed.
short_description: This spell will affect random plants within range of the spell to grow thorny tendrils and attack anyone who walks close to them.

description: This spell creates a woody pod in the caster’s hand. The caster must throw the pod within 12R or it will rupture in their hand and its effects occur around the caster. Once thrown the pod will travel up to 20Sd through the air and may bounce a further 20Sd unless it hits an obstruction. The accuracy is determined by the caster’s throw skill. Once the pod comes to rest it will rupture releasing a rapidly spreading network of pale fungal filaments, spreading up a 4Sd radius over the course of 1R. Anyone caught in the area of effect will find the filaments creeping up under their clothing and armour (unless they are magically protected in some way). The filaments will cause a terrible itching and burning sensation on the skin causing 1HP of damage. The following round toadstools and other fungal flowering heads will begin to develop across the whole area. This will include on the bodies of people or animals within it (60% chance of such a growth occurring on their body). The fungi themselves will have no effect unless disturbed when they will emit a cloud of choking poison spores in a 1Sd radius. Anyone caught in such a cloud must make a luck roll to avoid breathing the spores. If they fail they will cough uncontrollably for 1D4 rounds, during which time all their skill rolls will be at half normal chance. Once infected with the spores the victim must make a VGR roll vs that of the disease which is 17. If the succeed the disease will lose a point of VGR, if they fail they will. The victim will develop fatigue and a chronic cough until they have beaten the disease or die. The patch of fungus will remain active for 1D4 days and anyone trying to pass through it must make an AGx3 roll to avoid disturbing any of the fungi.
short_description: This spell creates a woody pod in the caster’s hand. The caster must throw the pod within 12R or it will rupture in their hand and its effects occur around the caster.

description: Causes plants to grow up in an area the caster indicates within range over course of 2R. The plants will form a hedge roughly 20Sd long, 3Sd high and 1Sd thick. Once grown the plants will become woody and thorny but still covered in green leaves. The hedge will have roughly 50HP in any given location and whilst the spell remains in effect any damaged areas will re-grow at a rate of 5HP each round. The hedge will take damage from fire, but a torch will be insufficient to out-damage the rate of re-growth. Magical fire or flames fed by an accelerant such as oil may cause enough damage each round to take hold. Once the spell ends the plants will become dead and brittle and may be broken through without difficulty.
short_description: Causes plants to grow up in an area the caster indicates within range over course of 2R. The plants will form a hedge roughly 20Sd long, 3Sd high and 1Sd thick.

description: Dismisses a lesser Plant Spirit. If cast at a greater or high Plant Spirit it will cause it to stop its actions for 1R.
short_description: Dismisses a lesser Plant Spirit. If cast at a greater or high Plant Spirit it will cause it to stop its actions for 1R.

description: Summons a greater plant spirit and enable it to manifest. Such spirits will often remain hidden within the trees which they inhabit. When they do show themselves they often appear as beautiful humanoid forms slight in stature trailing leaves and flower petals where they pass. Such spirits will attack a foe if asked, often tripping people with tangled roots, or throwing fruit, fir cones or nuts. They may also drop deadwood from the tree canopy on someone beneath, (which may do up to 1D20 damage, but only has a 40% chance of hitting). The spirits may also attempt to get enemies lost by creating intoxicating perfumes to lead them off the path. Greater plant spirits will also act as forest guides, scouts or if asked about happenings in the locale they may attempt to mime answers.
short_description: Summons a greater plant spirit and enable it to manifest. Such spirits will often remain hidden within the trees which they inhabit.

description: Enables the caster and their non-magical accoutrements to turn into a twisted creeping vine, able to grow up any surface in any direction that they choose. They may grow at a rate of 3Sd per round outwards in all directions putting roots into walls and sending tendrils up to 3Sd to span gaps. Whilst water will not harm the vine, it cannot grow down into it by more than 1Lk. The caster may see and hear in this form but cannot speak. They may cast plant spells. The vine has fine tendrils but the stalks become tough and woody making it generally immune to bludgeon and impale attacks but taking half damage from cutting attacks. However even if the vine is hacked to pieces as long as there is one section which still retains a root-hold somewhere the caster will be able to recover. As a rough guide the caster will lose a percentage of their overall VGR for the percentage of vine cut down. So if half the vine was cleared the recipient would lose half their VGR.
short_description: Enables the caster and their non-magical accoutrements to turn into a twisted creeping vine, able to grow up any surface in any direction that they choose.

description: Dismisses a lesser or greater Plant Spirit. If cast at a high Plant Spirit the spell will cause it to stop its actions for 1R.
short_description: Dismisses a lesser or greater Plant Spirit. If cast at a high Plant Spirit the spell will cause it to stop its actions for 1R.

description: Summons a High Plant Spirit. Such a spirit is a god or goddess of the particular forest that the caster is in and, as such, very powerful. They are generally fey and dangerous and may demand bargains of more than a mere gift of key for their help. The caster may ask them any questions about the forest, they may also ask for swift passage up to 5 mille from their current location in a matter of minutes (the spirit may be bargained with to carry them further, or for more people). The spirit may also be asked to capture or attack an enemy, which it may do so for six rounds. It should be noted that such a spirit has free will, and if asked to attack someone it favours, it may well just vanish or even turn upon the caster.
short_description: Summons a High Plant Spirit. Such a spirit is a god or goddess of the particular forest that the caster is in and, as such, very powerful.

description: Causes random plants in a wide ring around the caster to grow thorny tendrils and attack anyone who walks close. Initially the ring will be in a 40Sd radius circle about of the point of casting. But the caster may push the width of the whole ring outwards creating an unaffected area in the centre. Doing this will reduce the width of the band of affected ground at the same time. Thus if the ring is pushed out to a 50Sd radius, a 20Sd radius unaffected area could be made in the centre but the width of the affected band would have been reduced to just 30Sd The chance of anyone in the area being close enough to an affected plant will be 20% each round. The attacks consist of 4 grabbing and scratching attacks each round. Each tendril will have 5+1D4 HP, and a STR of 2D4+4. They may make two grab and two scratch attacks each round, as follows: Weapon attack damage parry Grab 50% * Scratch 50% 1D4+3 *The grab attack will do no damage, but will hold the victim fast, unless they break free in a STR vs. STR struggle or the tendril is cut. The victim should make a luck roll to determine whether they have an arm free when grabbed.
short_description: Causes random plants in a wide ring around the caster to grow thorny tendrils and attack anyone who walks close. Initially the ring will be in a 40Sd radius circle about of the point of casting.

description: Dismisses a lesser, greater or high Plant spirit. It should be noted that whilst this spell enables the caster to command a lesser or greater spirit, it gives them no such power over a high plant spirit. Whilst the high spirit is bound to obey the spell, if it did not wish to leave of its own free will, it may cause problems for the caster whilst they remain within its demesne.
short_description: Dismisses a lesser, greater or high Plant spirit.
Spell Glyphs: Abon + Maef + Ghan + Ovet + Eyama + Chom

description: Increases the Size attribute of the recipient beast in all proportions by 2 points. This will include any items worn like saddles or fitted armour, but not usually weapons or baggage. The beast’s Hit Points will also increase by 1 point for the duration of the spell. Any HP damage taken during the spell will be retained after it ends.
short_description: Increases the Size attribute of the recipient beast in all proportions by 2 points. This will include any items worn like saddles or fitted armour, but not usually weapons or baggage.

description: Decreases the SIZ attribute of the recipient beast in all proportions by 2 points. This will include clothing worn by the recipient, but not such items as weapons or baggage. As a result of the decrease in SIZ, the recipient’s HP will be reduced by 1 point for the duration of the spell.
short_description: Decreases the SIZ attribute of the recipient beast in all proportions by 2 points. This will include clothing worn by the recipient, but not such items as weapons or baggage.

description: Subtly alters the appearance of the target beast. This can include small random changes to face-shape, markings and fur colouring. If the beast has a LKS attribute then 1D6 should be rolled. If the number rolled is 1-3 then the beast’s LKS will go down by that amount. If the number is 4 or higher, then deduct 3 and add that to the beast’s LKS. Once the spell ends the beast will return to its normal appearance.
short_description: Subtly alters the appearance of the target beast. This can include small random changes to face-shape, markings and fur colouring.

description: Enables the caster to sense the presence of any beasts, of SIZ 2 or greater, within range. The caster will receive a vague impression of distance direction and approximate size but not species.
short_description: Enables the caster to sense the presence of any beasts, of SIZ 2 or greater, within range. The caster will receive a vague impression of distance direction and approximate size but not species.

description: If cast generally, a random creature within a 1 mille range will feel the call to come to the caster. It may take up to 1D20 minutes for the creature to reach the caster. Alternatively specific creature may be targeted if the caster chooses, provided it is within 20Sd. To successfully call a creature the caster must overcome the beast’s KEY. A beast of an intelligent species may make an INTx5 roll to resist the summons. Once the beast has reached the caster it will be in full control of its own actions and will probably be wondering what it is doing there. If it is an aggressive or frightened it may attack any persons it sees as a threat or prey, including the caster.
short_description: If cast generally, a random creature within a 1 mille range will feel the call to come to the caster. It may take up to 1D20 minutes for the creature to reach the caster.

description: Causes the recipient (or the caster) to grow additional claws, fangs or horns (at the Narrator’s discretion) which can be used to attack for the spell’s duration. The beast weapons will cause 1D6 damage each time they hit and may be used at basic chance or at whatever skill the recipient has with these weapons. NB this spell can be used either on a human or beast.
short_description: Causes the recipient (or the caster) to grow additional claws, fangs or horns (at the Narrator’s discretion) which can be used to attack for the spell’s duration.

description: Enables the caster to gain increased visual sensitivity associated with animals that are normally preyed upon. This gives the caster an almost 360 field of vision at the expense of some depth perception. The caster’s ability to detect movement will also be increased, giving +40% to see rolls where movement is involved. The reduced depth perception will reduce missile weapon skills by 20%.
short_description: Enables the caster to gain increased visual sensitivity associated with animals that are normally preyed upon.

description: Allows the caster to mimic the call of any beast that they have heard before (provided they have reasonable recall). As many different calls can be made as the caster desires whilst the spell is still in effect. Although, indiscriminate calling of certain beasts has been known to summon other rival beasts of the same species.
short_description: Allows the caster to mimic the call of any beast that they have heard before (provided they have reasonable recall).

description: Heals 1D4 Hit Points for one specific wound that a beast has suffered. It is recommended that the wound be prepared first using normal healing arts to set the wound right and prevent infection. Once a wound has received healing in this way, additional casting of the spell will not have any further effect. If the caster uses Key stored in Arbine to fuel the spell they may double the HP healed on that wound. It should be noted that the doubling effect of Arbine will not work for spells cast from stored spell objects.
short_description: Heals 1D4 Hit Points for one specific wound that a beast has suffered. It is recommended that the wound be prepared first using normal healing arts to set the wound right and prevent infection.

description: Causes 1D4 hit points of damage to the target beast through a strange type of decay on the location touched. The caster must overcome the beast’s KEY for this to be effective.
short_description: Causes 1D4 hit points of damage to the target beast through a strange type of decay on the location touched. The caster must overcome the beast’s KEY for this to be effective.

description: Allows the caster to conceal themselves amongst beasts. It also reduces any disturbance their appearance scent and actions might cause to the beasts. The spell is very effective amongst a herd of creatures. When still or moving with the herd the caster will only be spotted on a critical see roll. If moving without the herd the caster will still only be spotted at half chance. This spell may be less effective with lone beasts or particularly aggressive creatures. It is unlikely to work on beasts that are attacking or in a state of excitement. Any beast may see through the concealment if they succeed in an INTx3 roll.
short_description: Allows the caster to conceal themselves amongst beasts. It also reduces any disturbance their appearance scent and actions might cause to the beasts.

description: This spell is similar in effect to Ovet-Eyama, however the beast summoned will generally be of a large and fierce variety, if the local area permits. The range for the spell is roughly 1 mille and it may take up to 20 minutes for the beast to reach the caster. A specific beast within 20Sd may be targeted if desired. The caster must overcome the beast’s KEY for the spell to take effect. A beast of an intelligent species may make an INTx5 roll to resist the summons. The spell will last for as long as it takes the beast to arrive plus 12R. During this time the beast will remain under the control of the caster. The beast summoned may be directed by simple commands to attack a foe, group of foes, or to guard a person or area, but little else. Once the spell has finished the beast should make an INTx5 roll. If it succeeds it will probably leave, if it fails it will continue to attack or guard although there is little to stop it from turning to attack the caster or anyone with them. NB: Casters should be prepared to find that they have summoned a shrew or even a stink snake. If there are no beasts in the area the spell may fail entirely.
short_description: This spell is similar in effect to Ovet-Eyama, however the beast summoned will generally be of a large and fierce variety, if the local area permits.

description: Creates a growth on the skin of the target beast. The growth will take roughly 12R to reach its full size. After this, should the beast become excited in some way, either through panic, being attacked or by attacking there is a chance each round of increased blood flow causing the swelling to burst. When this happens it will spray foul-smelling pus over an area up to 2Sd away. Anyone who comes into contact with this pus must roll under their VGR/CON or contract the disease. The disease manifests rapidly causing unsightly sores and pustules around the mouth and eyes. It will also affect the victim’s ability to think straight as the disease progresses. The disease starts with a VGR or 25 which will drop by 1 point each minute after infection. Each minute the victim may attempt to roll their VGR vs the VGR of the disease. If they fail the disease will progress and the victim will lose a point of INT and a point of LKS/CHA. If they succeed, the disease will lose a further point of its VGR and will progress no further that minute (a critical roll will automatically reduce the VGR of the disease to 0). Once the VGR of the disease has been reduced to 0 the victim will begin to recover their lost attributes at a rate of 1 point each hour.
short_description: Creates a growth on the skin of the target beast. The growth will take roughly 12R to reach its full size.

description: Causes a hairy, horned, fanged, shell or carapace-like growth to sprout from the recipient’s forearms. The growths may be used to block or parry an attack at their basic parry chance or whatever skill they have developed specific to this form of defence. Similar to a normal shield, the beast shield offers up to 10 points of protection. If this is exceeded when parrying a critical blow then any extra damage will be taken by the recipient. However, the beast shield will regrow any damage it takes by the next round. If it is used as a weapon the beast shield will cause 1D6 damage. Again, this will be at the recipient’s basic attack chance unless they have developed a skill specific to this spell. Once the spell is ended the beast shield will retract back into the recipient’s body.
short_description: Causes a hairy, horned, fanged, shell or carapace-like growth to sprout from the recipient’s forearms.

description: Summons one or more small to large creatures from the surrounding area to stand guard over the caster, or a place indicated by the caster. The type will depend on the area and can be at the discretion of the GM. In some places there may be no creatures in range and the spell will fail. The following may be used as a rough guide or to roll randomly: 1 small burrowing creatures 2 small birds 3 medium scavenger or herbivore 4 medium birds 5 large bird of prey 6 larger herbivore 7 larger scavenger 8 larger predator Should someone or something approach (who was not already with the caster) the creature(s) will give some form of alarm call to alert the caster of the danger. Whilst the defending creatures will make every effort to appear aggressive and try to frighten off the threat, it will be unlikely to attack unless it in the nature of that type of creature to do so. Once the spell ends the creature(s) will most likely go back to their normal habits.
short_description: Summons one or more small to large creatures from the surrounding area to stand guard over the caster, or a place indicated by the caster.

description: Conceals a beast or creature by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle. This effectively gives the creature a 100% hide, especially if viewed from one angle from some distance. The illusion will remain completely convincing until either the viewer or the beast moves, when the camouflage briefly ceases to match what is behind and must re-orient itself on the following round. If there is more than one viewer the illusion will struggle to create multiple background colours that will begin to overlap to meet each separate viewpoint rendering the concealment less effective.
short_description: Conceals a beast or creature by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle.

description: Enables the caster to create an illusion of a particular beast or group of beasts occupying an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions. The illusion may be a snapshot of the beast or beasts within range of the spell as indicated by the caster during casting. If no area is indicated or available, the illusion will be of generic creatures common to that area. The illusion will not move but will be convincingly three dimensional until walked through.
short_description: Enables the caster to create an illusion of a particular beast or group of beasts occupying an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions.

description: Ceates a pouch within the recipient beast’s body. Penetrating between layers of muscle or internal organs. The pouch is flexible and generally accessed through a natural crease crease or fold in the skin. How much the pouch can contain depends upon its location. The abdomen is considered best, though any fleshy parts are suitable. When the spell ends anything the pouch contains will be pushed out through the opening before it disappears.
short_description: Ceates a pouch within the recipient beast’s body. Penetrating between layers of muscle or internal organs.

description: Heals 1HP, +1HP per level on one specific open wound of an injured beast. Even if all the damage is not fully healed the wound will close and the skin heal over without any scarring. As with the Ovet-Ix spell, a healing skill roll should be attempted first to avoid any infection. If the caster uses arbine, then they may double the effect to heal 2HP, +2HP per level.
short_description: Heals 1HP, +1HP per level on one specific open wound of an injured beast. Even if all the damage is not fully healed the wound will close and the skin heal over without any scarring.

description: Causes the target beast to grow larger in all proportions. If the beast is unwilling, the caster must first overcome its KEY. The spell effectively increases the SIZ of the target beast by 2 points per level of the caster. Consequently the HP of the beast will also go up by 1 point for every level of the caster.
short_description: Causes the target beast to grow larger in all proportions. If the beast is unwilling, the caster must first overcome its KEY.

description: Causes the target beast to become smaller in all proportions. If the beast is unwilling, the caster must first overcome its KEY. The spell effectively decreases the SIZ of the target beast by 2 points per level of the caster. Consequently the HP of the beast will also go down by 1 point for every level of the caster. The beast cannot shrink to less than SIZ 1, but can effectively be a smaller SIZ 1 ie the difference between a mouse and a cricket.
short_description: Causes the target beast to become smaller in all proportions. If the beast is unwilling, the caster must first overcome its KEY.

description: Enables the caster to draw sculpted marks on the skin of a creature using their hand and fingers. These marks resemble deep scarification but will not cause the recipient beast any discomfort. If the beast is heavily furred the fur will follow the pattern of the marks. The caster may even make marks on a creature’s shell, horns or carapace. When the spell ends the beast’s skin will return to normal.
short_description: Enables the caster to draw sculpted marks on the skin of a creature using their hand and fingers. These marks resemble deep scarification but will not cause the recipient beast any discomfort.

description: Causes the target beast have difficulty moving in a particular direction for the duration of the spell. The beast will have its AG halved when moving in the direction determined by the caster at the time of casting. This can be a simple compass direction, up or down or even something like “towards me”. Any other directions will have no effect on the beast’s ability to move.
short_description: Causes the target beast have difficulty moving in a particular direction for the duration of the spell.

description: Heals 2D4 Hit Points on an injured beast. A healing roll should be made first to prepare any wounds and prevent infection. If the caster uses key from Arbine they may double the HP healed. The spell will affect overall Hit Points and all wounds will benefit. Wounds partially healed with this spell will not benefit from further casting.
short_description: Heals 2D4 Hit Points on an injured beast. A healing roll should be made first to prepare any wounds and prevent infection.

description: Causes 1D4 Hit Points damage, +1 extra Hit Point per level, to the target creature through a strange type of decay appearing at various points on the beast’s body. The spell will not work through armour or clothing. It is necessary for the caster to overcome the beast’s Key for the spell to take effect.
short_description: Causes 1D4 Hit Points damage, +1 extra Hit Point per level, to the target creature through a strange type of decay appearing at various points on the beast’s body.

description: Causes a random variety of beast (usually a small common animal) from the local area to come to the caster to the caster’s call. A beast of an intelligent species may make an INTx5 roll to resist the summons. Once it has been summoned the beast will remain docile for the duration of the spell and may be issued simple commands similar to those given to a dog (depending upon the intelligence of the beast in question). Once the spell has ended the beast will be in control of its own actions and will probably leave. If it perceives the caster or anyone else in its way as a threat there is a chance that it may attack. NB if there are no beasts within range the spell may fail. The caster should also be prepared to find that they have summoned a mouse or even a fish.
short_description: Causes a random variety of beast (usually a small common animal) from the local area to come to the caster to the caster’s call.

description: Allows the caster to see through the eyes of the target beast should it fail to resist. If the beast has a see in the dark skill then the caster will be able to use it. The view cannot be altered by the caster, they will only see what the beast wishes to look at.
short_description: Allows the caster to see through the eyes of the target beast should it fail to resist. If the beast has a see in the dark skill then the caster will be able to use it.

description: Protects the recipient beast from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at them up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 symbol spells will be repelled without affecting this spell. 3 symbol spells or the attacks of lesser elementals will only be warded for 1R before this spell fails. NB this counter spell is only really effective against spells which are aimed at the protected individual, spells which cause harm through a secondary physical effect such as creating a gas cloud may not be cancelled. Neither will the spell allow the beast to cancel a spell which is running on something or someone else simply by touching it. However the GM may wish to make an exception in the case of any of the Element-XAMA-CYA spells which as a rule lie dormant until the victim’s proximity sets them off.
short_description: Protects the recipient beast from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at them up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 symbol spells will be repelled without affecting this spell.

description: Allows the caster to use a small creature such as an insect, finger snake or small bird, nothing larger than a rat, to seed a hand sized cocoon. The cocoon can be thrown (within 12R) towards a target area, flying up to 100Sd at a rate of 50Sd each round. As it travels, the cocoon will expand into a writhing mass of fur, feathers, or bugs, depending on what creature was used for the source. Once it reaches the target area the mass will burst apart into a thick swarm of the creatures. The swarm will indiscriminately attack anyone within a 5Sd radius of the target area. The attacks of such creatures can be determined by the Narrator. Much of the effect of such a swarm is likely to be psychological. The accuracy of the spell is as follows: range chance to hit target 20Sd 100% 50Sd 75% 100Sd 50% The creatures in the swarm will remain in the target area for the duration of the spell after which the will weaken and drop dead.
short_description: Allows the caster to use a small creature such as an insect, finger snake or small bird, nothing larger than a rat, to seed a hand sized cocoon.

description: Turns the beast upon which it is cast invisible to normal eyesight. The beast can move, attack, or cast spells (if capable) without being seen. The invisibility covers the surface of the beast only; any fluids or food entering the body will become invisible, whilst any wounds, or blood etc. will become visible. NB some animals may become alarmed or disoriented if they cannot see themselves. If it succeeds in an INTx3 roll it may panic. The invisibility does not extend to infrared or chimeric light.
short_description: Turns the beast upon which it is cast invisible to normal eyesight. The beast can move, attack, or cast spells (if capable) without being seen.

description: Conceals the magical aura given off by the caster, as well as any spells or magical objects which they have upon them. The spell will effectively hide them from most magical detection spells or scrying. The spell will also protect the caster from setting off any XAMA CYA spells, as these rely on a victim’s aura to be triggered.
short_description: Conceals the magical aura given off by the caster, as well as any spells or magical objects which they have upon them.

description: Changes the target beast into a human of equivalent SIZ. The human will still retain the mind and KEY of the original beast, although it may be able to learn quickly if the human form has a high enough INT. It is unlikely that the recipient will posses the power of speech, though beasts that spend a lot of time around people may have a simple grasp of language. Note that if the beast returns to its normal form it will retain its human memories with a beast-like understanding, although if the spell is cast again it will recall them perfectly.
short_description: Changes the target beast into a human of equivalent SIZ.

description: Enables the caster and their accoutrements to transform in to a swarm of small creatures such as butterflies, ants, rats birds or similar. The exact type can be anything within reason. The swarm will move at roughly twice the caster’s normal move or a move appropriate to the species (slugs for example). Whilst individual members of the swarm move independently, they are all under the common control of the caster’s mind, if the caster wishes to move left they will all move left in their way. It is not possible to send one off on its own little scouting mission. Also, whilst the creatures may look like they have the natural weapons for that species, the bites and stings will be ineffective and cause little or no damage (although swarming at someone and nibbling them is still quite convincing). In this form the caster can see and hear as normal. They may cast any spells involving the beast symbol. If a sufficient percentage of the swarm is damaged or killed, the caster will begin to take damage to their VGR. The swarm needs to coalesce at the end of the spell for the caster to return to their normal form. The percentage of the swarm prevented from doing so will be how much VGR the caster loses in the process.
