short_description: Enables the caster and their accoutrements to transform in to a swarm of small creatures such as butterflies, ants, rats birds or similar. The exact type can be anything within reason.

description: Increases the Size attribute of the recipient in all proportions by 2 points. This will include any items worn by the recipient, but not usually weapons or baggage. The recipient’s Hit Points will also increase by 1 point for the duration of the spell. Any HP damage taken during the spell will be retained after it ends.
short_description: Increases the Size attribute of the recipient in all proportions by 2 points. This will include any items worn by the recipient, but not usually weapons or baggage.

description: Decreases the SIZ attribute of the recipient or object in all proportions by 2 points. This will include clothing worn by the recipient, but not such items as weapons or baggage. As a result of the decrease in SIZ, the recipient’s HP will be reduced by 1 point for the duration of the spell.
short_description: Decreases the SIZ attribute of the recipient or object in all proportions by 2 points. This will include clothing worn by the recipient, but not such items as weapons or baggage.

description: Subtly alters the appearance of the recipient. This can include small random changes to face-shape, skin marks and shading, as well as hair type. This will affect the LKS attribute of the recipient and 1D8 should be rolled. If the number rolled is 1-4, then the person’s LKS will go down by that amount. If the number is 5 or higher, then deduct 4 and add that to the recipient’s LKS. Once the spell ends the recipient’s appearance will return to normal.
short_description: Subtly alters the appearance of the recipient. This can include small random changes to face-shape, skin marks and shading, as well as hair type.

description: Summons one humanoid to the caster as if called by an alarm or great need. The humanoid will usually be a person with low intelligence and key from nearby (other than those already in their presence) but could be up to 1 Mille from the caster (if there are none nearer). The caster must overcome the individual’s KEY for the spell to take effect. The caster may attempt to call a specific person in range by concentrating on that individual, but if they cannot overcome their KEY they will not register the summons. Anyone so summoned may attempt to resist responding to the call if they roll under INTx3. The person will not be in any kind of trance and may tell others about the call they have heard. Once they have arrived they will be in full control of their senses and will probably be wondering what the alarm was about. If there are no people within range of the caster the spell will fail.
short_description: Summons one humanoid to the caster as if called by an alarm or great need.

description: Allows the caster to detect the presence of any humanoid creatures within range of the spell. The enchanter will gain an impression of the approximate compass location and the general number of humanoids, even if they concealed or behind walls or floors, within the range of the spell.
short_description: Allows the caster to detect the presence of any humanoid creatures within range of the spell.

description: Enables the recipient to do +1 damage with natural weapons for the duration of the spell.
short_description: Enables the recipient to do +1 damage with natural weapons for the duration of the spell.

description: Will not protect the recipient from an attack; however it will make them less likely to be attacked than someone else standing next to them. If the recipient attacks someone first then that person will fight back. But if they choose not to attack anyone, and they are not seen to be casting offensive spells, then they will probably be left alone if there are other targets available. If a player character meets someone using this spell in combat, they would make a KEY vs KEY roll. If they fail then they will simply choose a more dangerous looking target, they can deal with the non-combatant later.
short_description: Will not protect the recipient from an attack; however it will make them less likely to be attacked than someone else standing next to them.

description: Enables the recipient to see just like a humanoid creature. Obviously if the caster is already a humanoid creature this spell will have little effect. However, if the caster is blinded in some way it may be a very helpful combination. It is at the Referee’s discretion if this spell would enable the caster to see through a blindfold or restrictive visor normally.
short_description: Enables the recipient to see just like a humanoid creature. Obviously if the caster is already a humanoid creature this spell will have little effect.

description: Allows the caster to mimic the voice and inflection of any person that they have heard. It will not give them the ability to speak, or understand a different language, but they can make an accent sound convincing.
short_description: Allows the caster to mimic the voice and inflection of any person that they have heard.

description: Heals 1D4 Hit Points on one specific wound that the recipient has suffered. It is recommended that the wound be prepared first using normal healing arts to set the wound right and prevent infection. If the caster uses Key stored in arbine to fuel the spell they may double the HP healed on that wound. Once a wound has received healing in this way, additional casting of the spell will not have any further effect. It should be noted that the doubling effect of arbine will not work for spells cast as stored spell objects.
short_description: Heals 1D4 Hit Points on one specific wound that the recipient has suffered. It is recommended that the wound be prepared first using normal healing arts to set the wound right and prevent infection.

description: Causes 1D4 hit points of damage to the target through a strange type of decay on the location touched. If Key from black arbine is used to fuel this spell it will cause double the damage.
short_description: Causes 1D4 hit points of damage to the target through a strange type of decay on the location touched. If Key from black arbine is used to fuel this spell it will cause double the damage.

description: Significantly alters the appearance of the recipient to match societal expectations of attractiveness. This can include changes to face-shape, skin marks and shading, as well as hair type. It can make an aged person look more healthy and youthful and can also change the appearance of someone’s body shape to have the right kind of curves or muscle tone. It will not change someone’s SIZ. This will effectively increase the LKS attribute of the recipient by 4+1D4 points. If the recipient’s LKS exceed the normal human maximum of 24 as a result, it may be obvious that their LKS are as a result of magic and people may be suspicious.
short_description: Significantly alters the appearance of the recipient to match societal expectations of attractiveness. This can include changes to face-shape, skin marks and shading, as well as hair type.

description: Significantly alters the appearance of the recipient to match societal expectations of unattractiveness. This can include changes to face-shape, skin marks and shading, as well as hair type. It can make someone look more aged or drawn and can also change the appearance of someone’s body shape to have the wrong kind of curves or muscle tone. It will not change someone’s SIZ. This will effectively decrease the LKS attribute of the recipient by 4+1D4 points.
short_description: Significantly alters the appearance of the recipient to match societal expectations of unattractiveness. This can include changes to face-shape, skin marks and shading, as well as hair type.

description: This enables the caster to give their appearance another person. If the target is unwilling the caster must first overcome their KEY. Once cast the caster’s own face, hair and skin colour will be transferred to the target. Their body shape will also become a close approximation of that of the caster. The spell will not alter the target’s SIZ attribute to match. It should be noted that if the caster already has magic upon them altering their appearance it will be the altered appearance that is transferred.
short_description: This enables the caster to give their appearance another person. If the target is unwilling the caster must first overcome their KEY.

description: Enables the caster to copy another person’s appearance. Once cast their own face, hair and skin colour will become a duplicate of the target’s. Their body shape will also become a close approximation of that of the target’s. The spell will not alter the caster’s SIZ attribute to match.
short_description: Enables the caster to copy another person’s appearance. Once cast their own face, hair and skin colour will become a duplicate of the target’s.

description: Enables the caster and a recipient to exchange their appearances. If the target is unwilling the caster must first overcome their KEY. Once cast their face, hair and skin colour will be transferred to the other. Their body shape will also become a close approximation of that of the other The spell will not alter either of their SIZ attributes to match. It should be noted that if the caster already has magic upon them altering their appearance it will be the altered appearance that is transferred.
short_description: Enables the caster and a recipient to exchange their appearances. If the target is unwilling the caster must first overcome their KEY.

description: Enables the recipient to hide amongst groups of people -to ‘blend in with the crowd’. Thus anyone attempting to look for them will not spot them as an individual unless they critical a see roll. A large group of 7 or more will not think anything odd about their extra companion. The caster must overcome the Key of each member of a smaller group to avoid anyone noticing. Should the recipient leave the crowd they will become noticeable as normal, but may hide themselves again should they re-join the crowd whilst the spell remains in effect.
short_description: Enables the recipient to hide amongst groups of people -to ‘blend in with the crowd’. Thus anyone attempting to look for them will not spot them as an individual unless they critical a see roll.

description: Enables the caster to attenuate their limbs and body during combat. This unexpected reach over an opponent gives the caster a +15% chance to hit whilst the spell is active. The added reach will also give the caster +2AG for combat rankings. This only works for close combat not missile fire. Any armour or clothing will appear to change shape with the caster’s limbs so that they remain protected and covered. This effect can only happens during combat, it cannot be used to reach items on the top shelf or pick someone’s pocket.
short_description: Enables the caster to attenuate their limbs and body during combat. This unexpected reach over an opponent gives the caster a +15% chance to hit whilst the spell is active.

description: Similar in effect to Nanu-Eyama; however the person summoned will generally be an armed fighter if the local area permits. The range for the spell is roughly 1 mille and it may take up to 20 minutes for the fighter to reach the caster. The caster must overcome the person’s KEY for the spell to have any effect. The caster may attempt to call a specific person in range by concentrating on that individual, but if they cannot overcome their KEY they will not register the summons. Anyone so summoned may attempt to resist responding to the call if they roll under INTx3. The spell will last for as long as it takes the fighter to arrive plus 12R.. The fighter summoned may be commanded to attack a foe, group of foes, or to guard a person or area. If the command is counter intuitive: for example to attack their own people the individual may attempt to make an INTx3 roll each round. If the command is particularly against reason or suicidal, the INT roll should be adjusted accordingly. Once the spell has finished the fighter should make an INTx5 roll each round . Once they succeed they will regain their full wits and may wonder why they have been fighting for the caster.
short_description: Similar in effect to Nanu-Eyama; however the person summoned will generally be an armed fighter if the local area permits.

description: Creates a growth on the skin of the target humanoid. The growth will take roughly 12R to reach its full size. After this, should the target become excited in some way; either through panic, being attacked, or by attacking; there is a chance each round of increased blood flow causing the swelling to burst. When this happens it will spray foul-smelling pus over an area up to 2Sd away. Anyone who comes into contact with this pus must roll under their VGR/CON or contract the disease. The disease manifests rapidly causing unsightly sores and pustules around the mouth and eyes. It will also affect the victim’s strength and agility as the disease progresses. The disease starts with a VGR of 25 which will drop by 1 point each minute after infection. Each minute the victim may attempt to roll their VGR/CON vs the VGR of the disease. If they fail the disease will progress and the victim will lose a point of STR and a point of AG/DEX. If they succeed, the disease will lose a further point of its VGR and will progress no further that minute (a critical roll will automatically reduce the VGR of the disease to 0). Once the VGR of the disease is reduced to 0 the victim will begin to recover their lost attributes at a rate of 1 point each hour.
short_description: Creates a growth on the skin of the target humanoid. The growth will take roughly 12R to reach its full size.

description: Causes a gristly and bony growth to sprout from the recipient’s forearms. The growths may be used to block or parry an attack at their basic parry chance or whatever skill they have developed specific to this form of defence. Similar to a normal shield, the meat shield offers up to 10 points of protection. If this is exceeded when parrying a critical blow then any extra damage will be taken by the recipient. However, the meat shield will regrow any damage it takes by the next round. If it is used as a weapon the meat shield will cause 1D6 damage. Again, this will be at the recipient’s basic attack chance, unless they have developed a skill specific to this spell. Once the spell is ended the meat shield will retract back into the recipient’s body.
short_description: Causes a gristly and bony growth to sprout from the recipient’s forearms.

description: Summons a humanoid from the surrounding area to watch over the caster, or a place indicated by the caster. To do this the caster must first overcome their will in a Key vs Key struggle. Should someone or something approach (who was not already with the caster) the guard will call out or wake the caster, alerting them to the danger. They may also try to scare the creature or attackers away or to simply be obstinate; however they are unlikely to risk themselves actually being attacked. Should they be put in real danger, they may make an INT x 5 roll to shake off the enchantment’s urge to guard. Once the spell ends the individual will most likely wish to go back to their normal routine, although some may question why they have been acting so strangely.
short_description: Summons a humanoid from the surrounding area to watch over the caster, or a place indicated by the caster. To do this the caster must first overcome their will in a Key vs Key struggle.

description: Conceals a recipient by rendering them the same colours as whatever lies behind them when viewed from any given angle. This effectively giving them a 100% hide chance, especially if viewed from one angle from some distance. The illusion will remain completely convincing until either the viewer or the recipient moves, when the camouflage briefly ceases to match what is behind and must re-orient itself on the following round. If there is more than one viewer the illusion will struggle to create multiple background colours that will begin to overlap to meet each separate viewpoint rendering the concealment less effective.
short_description: Conceals a recipient by rendering them the same colours as whatever lies behind them when viewed from any given angle.

description: Enables the caster to create an illusion of a particular person or group of people occupying an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions. The illusion may be a snapshot of an individual or group within range of the spell as indicated by the caster during casting. If no people are indicated or available, the illusion will be of generic folk common to that area. The illusion will not move but will be convincingly three dimensional until walked through.
short_description: Enables the caster to create an illusion of a particular person or group of people occupying an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions.

description: Creates a pouch within the recipient’s body. Penetrating between layers of muscle or internal organs. The pouch is flexible and generally accessed through a natural crease or fold in the skin. How much the pouch can contain depends upon its location. The abdomen is considered best, though any fleshy part of the body is suitable. When the spell ends anything the pouch contains will be pushed out through the opening before it disappears.
short_description: Creates a pouch within the recipient’s body. Penetrating between layers of muscle or internal organs.

description: Heals 2D4 Hit Points on one specific open wound of an injured humanoid. Even if all the damage is not fully healed the wound will close and the skin heal over without any scarring. As with the NANU-Ix spell a healing skill roll should be attempted first to avoid any infection. If the caster uses arbine, then they may double the effect to heal 2HP, +2HP per level.
short_description: Heals 2D4 Hit Points on one specific open wound of an injured humanoid. Even if all the damage is not fully healed the wound will close and the skin heal over without any scarring.

description: Makes the target grow larger in all proportions. This will include any items worn by the target, but not usually weapons or baggage. If the target is unwilling, the caster must first overcome their KEY. The spell effectively increases the SIZ of the target by 2 points per level of the caster. Consequently the HP of the target will also go up by 1 point for every level of the caster.
short_description: Makes the target grow larger in all proportions. This will include any items worn by the target, but not usually weapons or baggage.

description: Makes the target smaller in all proportions. This will include any items worn by the target, but not usually weapons or baggage. If the target is unwilling, the caster must first overcome their KEY. The spell effectively decreases the SIZ of the target by 2 points per level of the caster. Consequently the HP of the target will also go down by 1 point for every level of the caster. The person cannot shrink to less than SIZ 1, but can effectively be a smaller SIZ 1 ie the difference between a mouse and a cricket.
short_description: Makes the target smaller in all proportions. This will include any items worn by the target, but not usually weapons or baggage.

description: Enables the caster to draw sculpted marks on the skin of a humanoid using their fingers. These marks resemble deep scarification but will not cause the recipient any discomfort. When the spell ends the recipient’s skin will return to normal.
short_description: Enables the caster to draw sculpted marks on the skin of a humanoid using their fingers. These marks resemble deep scarification but will not cause the recipient any discomfort.

description: Causes the target humanoid to have difficulty moving in a particular direction for the duration of the spell. The target will have their AG halved when moving in the direction determined by the caster at the time of casting. This feels like moving against a powerful opposing wind or other force. The affected direction can be a simple compass direction, up or down or even something like “towards me”. Any other direction will have no effect on the target’s ability to move.
short_description: Causes the target humanoid to have difficulty moving in a particular direction for the duration of the spell.

description: Heals 1D6+2 Hit Points on an injured humanoid. A healing roll should be made first to prepare any wounds and prevent infection. If the caster uses arbine they may double the HP healed. The spell will affect overall Hit Points and all wounds will benefit. Once wounds have been partially healed with this spell they will not benefit from further casting. New wounds received, however, will. It should be noted that the doubling effect of arbine will not work for spells cast as stored spell objects.
short_description: Heals 1D6+2 Hit Points on an injured humanoid. A healing roll should be made first to prepare any wounds and prevent infection.

description: Causes 2D4 HP damage to the target through a strange type of decay at various places on their body. The spell will not work through armour or clothing and it is also necessary for the caster to overcome the target’s Key. If the caster uses Black Arbine , they may double the effect of the spell.
short_description: Causes 2D4 HP damage to the target through a strange type of decay at various places on their body.

description: Allows the caster to see through the eyes of the target. The recipient need not be of the same humanoid species as the caster, providing it has some sort of eye. If the target is unwilling/ unaware their KEY must first be overcome. The target need not remain in range after the spell has been cast. The caster must close their own eyes for the spell to be effective.
short_description: Allows the caster to see through the eyes of the target. The recipient need not be of the same humanoid species as the caster, providing it has some sort of eye.

description: Protects the recipient from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at them up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 glyph spells will be repelled without affecting this spell. 3 glyph spells or the attacks of lesser elementals will only be warded for 1R before this spell fails. NB this counter spell is only really effective against spells which are aimed at the protected individual. Spells which cause harm through a secondary physical effect such as creating a gas cloud may not be cancelled. Neither will the spell allow the individual to cancel a spell which is running on something or someone else simply by touching it. However the referee may wish to make an exception in the case of any of the Element-Xama-Cya spells, which as a rule lie dormant until the victim’s proximity sets them off.
short_description: Protects the recipient from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at them up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 glyph spells will be repelled without affecting this spell.

description: Enables the caster to turn a humanoid target, and their non-magical accoutrements, into a beast. The caster may use the spell on themselves if desired. Only a prime enchanter may predetermine which beast the target will be transformed into. The beast form will not vary by more than 5 points in SIZ from the recipient’s normal SIZ. The beast still retain the mind and KEY of the recipient although their power of speech will be affected. They may only use magic associated with the beast symbol. They will have their own normal Hit points though they will gain any natural armour or weapons that such a beast normally would, although their skill to use them may only be at basic. objects which they have upon them. The spell will effectively hide them from most magical Nanu Ghan Eja Chom detection spells or scrying. The spell will also protect the caster from setting off any XAMA CYA spells, as these 4 V 2 touch rely on a victim’s aura to be triggered. Turns the recipient invisible to normal eyesight. The recipient can move, attack, or cast spells (if capable) without being seen. The invisibility covers the surface of the recipient only; any fluids or food entering the body will become invisible, whilst any wounds, or blood etc. will become visible. The invisibility does not extend to infrared or chimeric light.
short_description: Enables the caster to turn a humanoid target, and their non-magical accoutrements, into a beast. The caster may use the spell on themselves if desired.

description: A more powerful general healing combination that Heals 1D6+4 Hit Points on an injured humanoid. A healing roll should be made first to prepare any wounds and prevent infection. If the caster uses arbine they may double the HP healed. The spell will affect overall Hit Points and all wounds will benefit. Any wounds that are partially healed with this spell will not benefit from further magical healing. It should be noted that the doubling effect of arbine will not work for spells cast as stored spell teshets.
short_description: A more powerful general healing combination that Heals 1D6+4 Hit Points on an injured humanoid. A healing roll should be made first to prepare any wounds and prevent infection.

description: Conceals the magical aura given off by the caster, as well as any spells or magical
short_description: Conceals the magical aura given off by the caster, as well as any spells or magical

description: Makes any food upon which the spell is cast taste appetising and satisfying, even though the food is not actually changed.
short_description: Makes any food upon which the spell is cast taste appetising and satisfying, even though the food is not actually changed.

description: Makes any food upon which the spell is cast taste dry, sour, thin or past its best in some manner, even though the food is not actually changed.
short_description: Makes any food upon which the spell is cast taste dry, sour, thin or past its best in some manner, even though the food is not actually changed.

description: The spell will have no visible effect on the food on which it is cast, but when eaten it will give a feeling of well-being, vitality or gladness of heart. This will not affect the consumer’s judgment and the effect will generally only last as long as the spell.
short_description: The spell will have no visible effect on the food on which it is cast, but when eaten it will give a feeling of well-being, vitality or gladness of heart.

description: Creates a delicious looking cake or delicacy before the caster. The food tastes as good as it looks, but being only magical it has no nutritional value and does not have the power to satisfy hunger.
short_description: Creates a delicious looking cake or delicacy before the caster. The food tastes as good as it looks, but being only magical it has no nutritional value and does not have the power to satisfy hunger.

description: Makes the target unable to eat for the duration of the spell. Trying to eat will cause a nauseous reaction and they will probably vomit the food back up. The caster must overcome the target’s KEY for the spell to take effect.
short_description: Makes the target unable to eat for the duration of the spell. Trying to eat will cause a nauseous reaction and they will probably vomit the food back up.

description: Enables the caster to ‘find’ edible food of some sort, berries, roots, game etc. within the range of 1 mille per level. The spell will also allow the caster to know if there is no such food available. It may also be used to determine if any food that the caster already has is good to eat.
short_description: Enables the caster to ‘find’ edible food of some sort, berries, roots, game etc. within the range of 1 mille per level.

description: Causes any items of food that the caster indicates in range to fly to the caster’s hand. The food must be easily holdable in one hand.
short_description: Causes any items of food that the caster indicates in range to fly to the caster’s hand. The food must be easily holdable in one hand.

description: Renders any food upon which it is cast poisonous whilst the spell remains in effect. The poison will have a strength of 1D6+10 points which will attack the VGR of the victim. A poison vs. VGR roll should be made each round after eating such food, if the victim resists a roll the poison will lose a point of potency, should the victim fail then they will loose a point of VGR/CON. Rolls will continue until either the poison or the victim’s VGR is reduced to zero. The victim will suffer internal discomfort whilst affected by the poison as well as agonising stomach cramps for 1R each time they lose a point of VGR. Once the spell finishes the poison will immediately stop causing damage to the victim. The caster may end the spell at any point whilst it is running.
short_description: Renders any food upon which it is cast poisonous whilst the spell remains in effect. The poison will have a strength of 1D6+10 points which will attack the VGR of the victim.

description: Protects the caster and his clothing from food stains and marks. More importantly, it will also protect the caster from food poisoning (sinterra) and will halve the effect of any oral poison that the caster might consume.
short_description: Protects the caster and his clothing from food stains and marks.

description: Makes any spoiled, rotten, or mouldy food become edible once again. There are limits on how effective this spell will be on food which has begun breaking down and turning into gloop, at the discretion of the referee.
short_description: Makes any spoiled, rotten, or mouldy food become edible once again.

description: Causes an item of prepared or preserved foodstuffs touched by the caster to turn rotten or mouldy. The item will become inedible within moments.
short_description: Causes an item of prepared or preserved foodstuffs touched by the caster to turn rotten or mouldy. The item will become inedible within moments.

description: Creates a feast of delicious looking food before the caster. Enough to feed many. The food smells and tastes as good as it looks, but much like GHE CYA the fairy food has no power to satisfy hunger or give nutrition to those consuming it. Indeed, one person could eat the entire feast without sating their appetite.
short_description: Creates a feast of delicious looking food before the caster. Enough to feed many.

description: This will make the food upon which it is cast look bland or even to resemble something inedible, making it difficult to see as food. Importantly the spell will also help to mask any aroma which the food might give off, which will deter pets or predators. This can be cast on prepared food or a sizeable store up to 4Sd in all directions. It may also be used to hide cooking smells from a kitchen or similar. Food with no smell is not very appetising and the caster may need to cancel the spell if the food is to be consumed.
short_description: This will make the food upon which it is cast look bland or even to resemble something inedible, making it difficult to see as food.

description: Similar to Ghe Xama, this combination will cause any food upon which it is cast to become poisonous whilst the spell remains in effect. The poison will have a strength of 1D10+10 points which will attack the VGR of the victim. A poison vs. VGR roll should be made each round after eating such food, if the victim resists a roll the poison will lose a point of potency, should the victim fail then they will loose a point of VGR. Rolls will continue until either the poison or the victim’s VGR is reduced to zero. Whilst affected by the poison the victim will suffer internal discomfort along with agonising stomach cramps for 1R each time they lose a point of VGR. Once the spell finishes the poison will immediately stop causing damage to the victim. The caster may end the spell at any point whilst it is running. Once the spell finishes the poison will immediately stop causing damage to the victim. The caster may end the spell at any point whilst it is running.
short_description: Similar to Ghe Xama, this combination will cause any food upon which it is cast to become poisonous whilst the spell remains in effect.

description: To be set upon an item of food such as a large fruit, a cake, cooked chicken, even a bowl of stew. Should anyone else disturb or come into contact with the food it will explode spraying the victim and anyone stood nearby with the food. Little or no damage will be caused by the explosion other than the mess. However, if the dish was hot, anyone within 1Sd may suffer 1D4 damage from scalding. Because the spell is very similar to creating a magic object the caster’s key spent will take a week to regenerate (unless the spell is triggered before that time). The weaponised food will remain active indefinitely waiting to be set off, even if it rots down to dust.
short_description: To be set upon an item of food such as a large fruit, a cake, cooked chicken, even a bowl of stew.

description: Allows the caster to preserve roughly a weeks worth of perishable food. The food will remain fresh for one week per level of the caster. This spell may also be used to preserve a severed limb or similar until the victim can find a healer.
short_description: Allows the caster to preserve roughly a weeks worth of perishable food. The food will remain fresh for one week per level of the caster.

description: Renders the caster immune to the effects of spoiled food and drink, infected food or any kind of oral poison (including food affected byFood XAMA and GHE XAMA LERRU). The efficacy of the spell to protect against food (or drink) that has been drugged is at the discretion of the referee.
short_description: Renders the caster immune to the effects of spoiled food and drink, infected food or any kind of oral poison (including food affected byFood XAMA and GHE XAMA LERRU).

description: Will conceal a stock of foodstuffs or even prepared food by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle. This effectively gives the food a 100% hide, especially if viewed from one angle from some distance. The illusion will remain completely convincing until the viewer moves, when the camouflage briefly ceases to match what is behind and must re-orient itself on the following round. If there is more than one viewer the illusion will struggle to create multiple background colours that will begin to overlap to meet each separate viewpoint rendering the concealment less effective.
short_description: Will conceal a stock of foodstuffs or even prepared food by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle.

description: Enables the caster to create the illusion of food over an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions if desired. The illusion may be a duplication of food stores elsewhere within range of the spell as indicated by the caster during casting. If no food is indicated or available, the illusion will be of generic food consumed in the area. It should be noted that such interpretations are subjective to the caster, if the caster is human, then human preserves and foodstuffs in containers and sacks will most likely appear, if the caster is some other species the illusion will reflect their diet. The illusion will be convincingly three dimensional until passed through.
short_description: Enables the caster to create the illusion of food over an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions if desired.

description: Protects the recipient and their clothing from food stains. The spell will protect against any stomach upsets caused by bad or infected food (including Sinterra). It will also protect against the effect of most poisons that they may ingest.
short_description: Protects the recipient and their clothing from food stains. The spell will protect against any stomach upsets caused by bad or infected food (including Sinterra).

description: This must be cast upon some item of food, a nut, a bag of grain, even a pie. Once cast all foodstuffs within 20Sd of the initial item will be warded against food attacking magic including GHE XAMA CYA, GHE YLG and GHE Ghan YLG. If the initial item is moved, the area protected will move with it. Should the focus item be eaten the spell will end.
short_description: This must be cast upon some item of food, a nut, a bag of grain, even a pie.

description: Whilst the spell is active this combination enables the caster to sculpt prepared food into any shape they can envisage. The spell will not create any extra food which will limit the size of the final result to the amount of food the caster had to start with. The spell will affect roughly a large table’s worth of prepared food. Once the spell finishes, the caster cannot manipulate the food further. It will, however, remain as sculpted.
short_description: Whilst the spell is active this combination enables the caster to sculpt prepared food into any shape they can envisage.

description: Creates enough food to sustain one person for one day. Unlike other spells, this magically created food will actually satisfy hunger. The food will normally appear as a lumpy nutritious stew in a trencher. At higher levels the quality of the food may be significantly enhanced as determined by the narrator -little bread rolls, pats of butter, sprigs of parsley etc.
short_description: Creates enough food to sustain one person for one day. Unlike other spells, this magically created food will actually satisfy hunger.

description: Enables the caster to turn food that has recently become mouldy or bad back into an edible state. As much food or stocks of preserved food as the caster can touch whilst the spell is active can be healed in this way. This spell is particularly effective in reversing the effect of GHE Ghan YLG.
short_description: Enables the caster to turn food that has recently become mouldy or bad back into an edible state.

description: This will indiscriminately cause all prepared and preserved foodstuffs within the spell’s range to rot and turn mouldy. Any food stocks affected will become inedible within 12R.
short_description: This will indiscriminately cause all prepared and preserved foodstuffs within the spell’s range to rot and turn mouldy. Any food stocks affected will become inedible within 12R.

description: Acts to ward all foodstuffs, both raw or prepared, in the spell’s range from magical attacks.
short_description: Acts to ward all foodstuffs, both raw or prepared, in the spell’s range from magical attacks.

description: Creates one portion of food for each level of the caster. Each portion will be enough for one person to live on for one day. Thus at level 4 a caster could provide a meal for four people. As with GHE-Ghan-CYA the food will normally appear as a lumpy nutritious stew in a trencher. At higher levels the food will be enhanced -little bread rolls and pats of butter with a sprig of parsley etc.
short_description: Creates one portion of food for each level of the caster. Each portion will be enough for one person to live on for one day.

description: This will indiscriminately cause all prepared and preserved foodstuffs within the spell’s range to rot and turn mouldy. Any food stocks affected will become inedible within 12R.
short_description: This will indiscriminately cause all prepared and preserved foodstuffs within the spell’s range to rot and turn mouldy. Any food stocks affected will become inedible within 12R.

description: Acts to ward all foodstuffs, both raw or prepared, in the spell’s range from Ghe glyph magical attacks. It will also ward the foodstuffs from other magics up to 4 points such as Raghe and Lucha spells that might spoil the food.
short_description: Acts to ward all foodstuffs, both raw or prepared, in the spell’s range from Ghe glyph magical attacks.

description: Makes an object appear eye-catching, fascinating, something worth examining in more detail. It will not make the object more valuable, beautiful or better in any way, it will simply make it seem worth examining compared to other objects.
short_description: Makes an object appear eye-catching, fascinating, something worth examining in more detail.

description: Makes an object seem mundane, dull or not worth the bother. If there is anything else to look at then a viewer’s eye will be drawn to that instead. This spell is an excellent way to smuggle things, or to be able to carry powerful items without drawing attention.
short_description: Makes an object seem mundane, dull or not worth the bother. If there is anything else to look at then a viewer’s eye will be drawn to that instead.

description: Doubles the effective hit points of an object for the duration of the spell.
short_description: Doubles the effective hit points of an object for the duration of the spell.

description: Causes a small random object to appear in the caster’s hands. The GM should roll on the equipment lists do determine the object or to choose something appropriate. It may be possible, if the caster makes an INTx3 roll, to concentrate on the thought of a particular object enough to influence what appears. The object will look realistic but will have less weight and strength than the real thing and will doubtless break if used for its apparent function. If it is struck it will shatter and the pieces will vanish before they land. Similarly once the spell finishes the object will vanish.
short_description: Causes a small random object to appear in the caster’s hands. The GM should roll on the equipment lists do determine the object or to choose something appropriate.

description: Halves effective hit points of the target object for the duration of the spell. This spell will not work on objects that have their own symbol such as weapons, armour or doors. However, it may work on certain components of those things, such as buckles, bow-strings or padlocks.
short_description: Halves effective hit points of the target object for the duration of the spell. This spell will not work on objects that have their own symbol such as weapons, armour or doors.

description: Allows the caster to locate an object that they are familiar with provided it is within range of the spell. The range can be up to 20Sd per level of the caster. The spell may also be used in combination with forms of magical scrying, enabling the range of the spell to be effective from the magical point of vision.
short_description: Allows the caster to locate an object that they are familiar with provided it is within range of the spell. The range can be up to 20Sd per level of the caster.

description: Enables the caster to cause an object, that the caster indicates to fly into their hand. The object must be of a size that the caster can easily hold in one hand. If the object is held down in some way–i.e. nails, glue or someone is holding it–the caster must match their KEY against the effective strength of the restraining force. If the object is a weapon it will fly in such a way as to avoid injuring the enchanter. The range the caster can summon objects from will be 2Sd per level.
short_description: Enables the caster to cause an object, that the caster indicates to fly into their hand. The object must be of a size that the caster can easily hold in one hand.

description: Creates a trap upon an object which will remain dormant until it is touched by anyone other than the caster. Once triggered a blinding flash of light and a loud bang will happen. This will not actually harm the object or the target (unless they have a weak heart) although it may leave a few sooty marks.
short_description: Creates a trap upon an object which will remain dormant until it is touched by anyone other than the caster. Once triggered a blinding flash of light and a loud bang will happen.

description: Protects the caster from being hurt by falling objects, thrown objects or from tripping over objects. Whilst this sounds like the ultimate ‘catch all’ spell, sadly it is not, the spirits interpret Teshet objects fairly specifically and do not include anything which would constitute a weapon at that moment (anything thrown or dropped with intent to harm), or anything that has its own symbol, like Abon or Gonde (so trees and avalanches are out). It will protect against a falling flower pot, or a mis-thrown pruning shear. It would protect against a falling great harp, but not against a falling great hound. It may protect against a falling statue, but not a stone. What it will protect from is very much for the GM to interpret. A useful spell certainly, but one that cannot be relied upon for every eventuality.
short_description: Protects the caster from being hurt by falling objects, thrown objects or from tripping over objects.

description: Restores 1HP per level of the caster to a damaged object. This may even be effective if the object has been broken into several pieces although the point at which an object is no longer a single repairable object must be determined by the GM. If this spell is cast using Key stored in Arbine the effect may be doubled.
short_description: Restores 1HP per level of the caster to a damaged object.

description: Causes 1HP damage per level of the caster to the target object by touching it. This spell will cause the affected area of the object to become cracked and marked. If the object is magical the spell will usually fail.
short_description: Causes 1HP damage per level of the caster to the target object by touching it. This spell will cause the affected area of the object to become cracked and marked.

description: Causes any objects that the caster indicates to fly into their hand. This will work on any objects within a range of 2Sd per level. The caster may do this as many times as they wish whilst the spell is in effect. The objects can be up to the size/weight of anything a normal person may hold in one hand. If the object is held down in some way i.e. nails, glue, or someone is holding it, the caster may match their KEY against the effective STR of the restraining force.
short_description: Causes any objects that the caster indicates to fly into their hand. This will work on any objects within a range of 2Sd per level.

description: makes an object appear shabbier, more humdrum and worthless than it actually is, provided the beholder fails to make a KEY resistance roll against the caster. This effectively makes object easy to overlook. The caster does not have to be present.
short_description: makes an object appear shabbier, more humdrum and worthless than it actually is, provided the beholder fails to make a KEY resistance roll against the caster.

description: Similar to Teshet-Xama, enabling the caster to place a trap upon a given object. The trap will remain dormant until someone other than the caster touches the object. Once triggered it will emit a bright flash of light and loud explosion and will also cause 1D10 points of damage to anyone within 1Sd and 1D6 to anyone within 2Sd. If the spell is cast permanently this will reset every round. The object may be blackened as a result but unless very fragile it is unlikely to be damaged.
short_description: Similar to Teshet-Xama, enabling the caster to place a trap upon a given object. The trap will remain dormant until someone other than the caster touches the object.

description: This will protect the recipient from being hurt by falling objects, tripping over objects, or thrown objects. Whilst this sounds like the ultimate catch all spell, sadly it is not, the spirits interpret ‘objects’ fairly specifically and do not include anything which would constitute a weapon. However it will protect against a falling flower pot, or stone (even if it had been helped), it will also protect against being hit by a flask of acid, if not the acid itself. It might even protect against a falling great harp, but would not protect against a falling great hound. What it will protect from is very much for the GM to interpret. A useful spell certainly but one that cannot be relied upon across the board.
short_description: This will protect the recipient from being hurt by falling objects, tripping over objects, or thrown objects.

description: Will conceal an object up to the size of a cart by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle. This effectively gives the object a 100% hide, especially if viewed from one angle from some distance. The illusion will remain completely convincing until either the viewer or the object moves, when the camouflage briefly ceases to match what is behind and must re-orient itself on the following round. If there is more than one viewer the illusion will struggle to create multiple background colours that will begin to overlap to meet each separate viewpoint rendering the concealment less effective.
short_description: Will conceal an object up to the size of a cart by rendering it the same colours as whatever lies behind it when viewed from any given angle.

description: Enables the caster to create an illusion of a particular object or group of objects occupying an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions. The illusion may be a snapshot of objects within range of the spell as indicated by the caster during casting. If no objects are indicated or available, the illusion will be of generic things common to that area: barrels, carts, statues, huts etc.. The illusion will not move but will be convincingly three dimensional until walked through.
short_description: Enables the caster to create an illusion of a particular object or group of objects occupying an area up to 4Sd across in all dimensions.

description: Enables the caster to ascertain the magical properties of a single object, if any. If the object works by enchanting the caster will usually determine quite accurately what the properties are. Even if the object works using some other type of magic the spirits will attempt to tell the caster as much as they can determine. Generally the caster will see/feel the symbols used somewhere upon the object they are examining. Or the spirits will try to guide the caster’s hands or thoughts on how to activate the object. This spell relies upon the knowledge of the elemental spirits and is not always successful, especially if the spells within the object are very high level and complex, or if the object is intrinsically protected from just such a spell (at the GM’s discretion). The success rates to get a rough idea of function are as follows: 95% for magic stones like those described above 80% for most enchanting spells 60% for objects utilising spirits 50% for objects using sorcery 40% or less for objects powered by unusual magic. A critical roll will provide detailed instructions on function. NB if the elemental spirit fails to determine the function, the spell cannot be re-cast on that object as the spirits that help the enchanter will still be none the wiser (at least not until the enchanter has improved their skill by experience). However another enchanter may get better results as they will have different helper spirits.
short_description: Enables the caster to ascertain the magical properties of a single object, if any. If the object works by enchanting the caster will usually determine quite accurately what the properties are.

description: Provides protection of a large object from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at it up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 symbol spells will be repelled without affecting this spell. 3 symbol spells or the attacks of lesser elementals will only be warded for 1R before this spell fails. Using this spell an object up to the size of a small ship, a moderate house or a tower may be protected. NB this counter spell is only really effective against spells which are aimed at the protected object, spells which cause harm through a secondary physical effect such as creating an ball of flame may not be cancelled.
short_description: Provides protection of a large object from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at it up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 symbol spells will be repelled without affecting this spell.

description: This creates a palm sized, smooth, clear gemstone in the palm of the caster’s hand. These are often called ‘magic teshets’ or simply ‘teshets’. In the military they may be referred to as ordnance. This magically created gemstone may be used for storing spells. Once created, the enchanter must continue to hold the gem in the palm of their hand against their casting symbol while they cast the spell, or spells, they wish to store into it. The drawback is that the spell will take up to a day for each glyph of the spell to be absorbed into the teshet. If the caster removes the teshet during this time then the spell is lost and the empty teshet will crumble. Another issue of having a teshet covering their casting symbol is that the enchanter will be prevented from casting further spells until the first spell has been absorbed by the teshet.Usually enchanters engaged in making a teshet will secure it to their hands using bandages or a special glove to avoid it falling out of their hand while they sleep, or at other times. Needless to say the process is time consuming and awkward, making the cost of teshets quite high. Once the spell has been absorbed into the teshet, it will remain within it until needed. The stone will often take on a hue associated with the element used in the magic. A trained enchanter may also be able to recognise the glyphs of the spell set into the matrix of the stone. Once such a stone has been created, anyone may then activate the spell stored within by simply by holding the stone in the palm of their hand and concentrating. The spell will then be cast from the stone as if the spell had been cast by them. Any offensive magic that must overcome a target’s KEY will require a KEY struggle from the bearer of the stone (not the original enchanter). Once all the stored spells in a teshet are used, it is will quickly lose its integrity and crumble to dust. It should also be noted that teshets do not remain stable forever. Teshets will usually last for a year without issue but will become less stable the older they become. Old military ordnance is carefully rotated to ensure fresh teshets are always being added while older ones are used or destroyed. Characters might want to be wary buying cheap teshets from unreliable sources. Once the teshet fails the spell(s) stored within will all be released at the same time If the enchanter is a master of a craft skill then this spell may also be used in conjunction with the Ked (Permanence) Glyph whilst making an object of the craft concerned. The teshet stone will appear set upon the object. It will still take a day for each glyph used in the spell to be stored and the object must remain in contact with the enchanter’s casting hand. Once created however the spell that is cast into the object may then be cast and the object will not crumble. On the contrary the bearer may put their own key into the object to cast the spell over and over.
short_description: This creates a palm sized, smooth, clear gemstone in the palm of the caster’s hand. These are often called ‘magic teshets’ or simply ‘teshets’.

description: Enables the caster to match their KEY-10 against the current HP of a target object within range. If the caster succeeds in the struggle then the object will break or become useless in some way. If they critical the roll then the object will shatter. Should they fumble the roll the caster should roll on the fumble table as normal but the objects HP will be permanently increased by two points. This spell will usually fail when used against magical objects.
short_description: Enables the caster to match their KEY-10 against the current HP of a target object within range. If the caster succeeds in the struggle then the object will break or become useless in some way.

description: Turns the object upon which it is cast invisible to normal eyesight. The can be touched and moved without affecting its invisibility. The effect only covers the surface of the object; anything placed inside it will become invisible, whilst anything removed from it will become visible. The invisibility does not extend to infrared or chimeric light.
short_description: Turns the object upon which it is cast invisible to normal eyesight. The can be touched and moved without affecting its invisibility.

description: If cast upon an ordinary unlocked door this combination will quietly lift whatever latch it has and open it.
short_description: If cast upon an ordinary unlocked door this combination will quietly lift whatever latch it has and open it.

description: When cast upon an ordinary door this spell will quietly close and latch it.
short_description: When cast upon an ordinary door this spell will quietly close and latch it.

description: If cast upon an ordinary door, locked or unlocked, this will quietly unlock it and open it. Should the door have a particularly good lock, one that would normally have a minus against a lock picking skill, then the caster’s magic skill will also have that minus to open that lock. If the caster fails to roll under this amount but still succeeds in their normal Enchanting skill chance then the spell will still be cast and the door will rattle, but it will fail to open. If a door has several such locks, one spell will open them all and only the lock with the biggest penalty will affect the caster’s skill.
short_description: If cast upon an ordinary door, locked or unlocked, this will quietly unlock it and open it.

description: If cast upon an ordinary door, this spell will quietly close it and lock it, provided it has a lock. If it has no lock, a door will be held fast as if it is locked for the duration of the spell.
short_description: If cast upon an ordinary door, this spell will quietly close it and lock it, provided it has a lock. If it has no lock, a door will be held fast as if it is locked for the duration of the spell.

description: Whilst the spell is active this enables the caster to detect any doorways, openings, wells or pits within range. This will include any portals that are concealed through mundane means. The caster will sense where such openings are located, even in complete darkness or underwater.
short_description: Whilst the spell is active this enables the caster to detect any doorways, openings, wells or pits within range. This will include any portals that are concealed through mundane means.

description: Causes a normal hinged door or gate to behave as if possessed. The door will attempt injury on anyone, other than the caster, who tries to use it. The door will fly open and slam shut seemingly at random. A normal type door will have an attack chance of 50% and cause 1D4 damage. Larger doors may be affected, and do considerably more damage, but with an appropriately reduced chance to hit.
short_description: Causes a normal hinged door or gate to behave as if possessed. The door will attempt injury on anyone, other than the caster, who tries to use it.

description: Renders a door much more resistant to damage (doubling its effective Hit Points) whilst the spell is in effect. Additionally, provided the door is locked, the chance of any lock-picking attempts will be halved.
short_description: Renders a door much more resistant to damage (doubling its effective Hit Points) whilst the spell is in effect.

description: Restores 2HP per level of the caster to a damaged door or gate. This may even be effective if the object has been broken on its hinges. The point at which a door is no longer repairable must be determined by the GM. If this spell is cast using Key stored in Arbine the effect may be doubled.
short_description: Restores 2HP per level of the caster to a damaged door or gate. This may even be effective if the object has been broken on its hinges.

description: Causes 1HP per level of the caster damage to the target door or gate by touching it. The affected area of the door will become cracked and marked. If the doorway is magically protected the spell will usually fail.
short_description: Causes 1HP per level of the caster damage to the target door or gate by touching it. The affected area of the door will become cracked and marked.

description: Makes a door, gateway or opening appear much shabbier, dingy and unused than it actually is. Effectively making it much easier to overlook. To disbelieve the illusion the beholder must make a KEY resistance roll against the caster. The caster does not have to be present.
short_description: Makes a door, gateway or opening appear much shabbier, dingy and unused than it actually is. Effectively making it much easier to overlook.

description: Creates a trap upon a door, gate, even a portcullis, which will remain dormant until someone comes into its proximity. Once triggered the portal will slam shut (or open) in order to cause maximum damage to the target. The door/gate will then continue to try to attack anyone approaching it for a further 12R. After this the spell will be expended. With this spell, a normal door may cause 1D6 damage. Whilst a full size portcullis might cause as much as 4D6+10. The damage that the affected portal should be determined as appropriate by the referee.
short_description: Creates a trap upon a door, gate, even a portcullis, which will remain dormant until someone comes into its proximity.

description: Makes the affected door or gate considerably tougher. The door’s HP will be doubled and the door will gain an effective magical armour of 10 points of protection. Only the caster may open or shut the door, to anyone else the door will seem to be jammed shut or locked.
short_description: Makes the affected door or gate considerably tougher. The door’s HP will be doubled and the door will gain an effective magical armour of 10 points of protection.

description: Turns an ordinary door or opening into a hidden door, by causing it to blend in with its surroundings. This spell works for doors, gateways, small cave mouths, even simple openings to alleyways. When viewed from any particular angle the door will most likely resemble the surrounding walls or stone and be almost indistinguishable from them with a 100% hide. If the viewer moves or the door is opened the illusion may be seen for what it is, its hide reduced to 50% (this may vary depending on the depth of the opening and the blandness of the surroundings), until the spell changes the colours to match the new viewpoint on the next round. If there are several viewers the illusion may be further compromised as the spell attempts to create multiple background camouflages.
short_description: Turns an ordinary door or opening into a hidden door, by causing it to blend in with its surroundings. This spell works for doors, gateways, small cave mouths, even simple openings to alleyways.

description: Enables the caster to create the illusion of a doorway or opening in a wall or surface, The portal can appear be up to 4Sd wide, high and deep if required. The illusion may be a duplication of another opening within range of the spell as indicated by the caster during casting. If no area is indicated or available, the illusion will be of generic doorway or opening that might be found in the area. The illusion will be convincingly three dimensional until passed through.
short_description: Enables the caster to create the illusion of a doorway or opening in a wall or surface, The portal can appear be up to 4Sd wide, high and deep if required.

description: This will create an opening in a wall where the caster touches at the time of casting. The material of the wall will retract to create a portal roughly 1Sd in diameter. Such a portal can be created in a stone or wooden wall up to 1Lk thick. Once the spell finishes the hole will retract over the course of 1R. It should be noted that this spell may not work through walls protected by blood silver or magical means.
short_description: This will create an opening in a wall where the caster touches at the time of casting. The material of the wall will retract to create a portal roughly 1Sd in diameter.

description: Jams or blocks a doorway, gate, opening, well or pit up to 2Sd across. If there is no door to jam it the opening will be blocked with any wood, rubble or other detritus that can be found nearby. The blockage will be neatly squared off, not merely piled up. Anyone trying to prise material from the opening will find it jumps straight back into the space left. A determined group of diggers may be able to breach the barrier over a period of time, but they will find the barrier starting to rebuild itself as soon as they stop digging. Once the spell ends the barrier will most likely crumble or collapse under its own weight.
short_description: Jams or blocks a doorway, gate, opening, well or pit up to 2Sd across. If there is no door to jam it the opening will be blocked with any wood, rubble or other detritus that can be found nearby.

description: Restores 2HP per level of the caster to a damaged door or gate. This may even be effective if the door or lock has been smashed open. If this spell is cast using Key stored in Arbine the effect may be doubled.
short_description: Restores 2HP per level of the caster to a damaged door or gate. This may even be effective if the door or lock has been smashed open.

description: Enables the enchanter to match their KEY against the current Hit Points of a target door within range. If the enchanter succeeds in the struggle then the door will be smashed open (a critical success will explode the door in splinters causing 1D6 damage to anyone stood within 1Sd of the opposing side).
short_description: Enables the enchanter to match their KEY against the current Hit Points of a target door within range.

description: Enables the caster to change the destination of a mystic portal by moving one node onward from the normal destination node. The spell must be cast after the activation spell Casan-Meaf-Eyama has been cast. Once the portal is deactivated it will revert to its normal destination.
short_description: Enables the caster to change the destination of a mystic portal by moving one node onward from the normal destination node.

description: Will instantly close an active mystical portal. The spell may be cast from either end of the portal link and will close both ends at the same time.
short_description: Will instantly close an active mystical portal. The spell may be cast from either end of the portal link and will close both ends at the same time.

description: Enables the caster to activate one of the mystical portals that dot the far flung reaches of the landscape. The magic requires 1D6 rounds establish a connection with the destination portal, after which the link will remain open for 12R. Once established the caster and any others to pass through in either direction until the spell ends.
short_description: Enables the caster to activate one of the mystical portals that dot the far flung reaches of the landscape.

description: Allows the caster to know the approximate direction and distance of the nearest mystical portal. This will be effective even for a portal many mille distant. If this spell is cast within 20Sd of a mystical portal, it will cause the portal to display a vague glowing outline enabling the caster to see where it is, the glow will continue for the duration of the spell.
short_description: Allows the caster to know the approximate direction and distance of the nearest mystical portal. This will be effective even for a portal many mille distant.

description: Provides protection for a mundane doorway, gate or even portcullis from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at it up to 3 points in strength. 1 and 2 symbol spells will be repelled without affecting this spell. 3 symbol spells or the attacks of lesser elementals will only be warded for 1R before this spell fails. NB this counter spell is only really effective against spells which are aimed at the protected object, spells which cause harm
short_description: Provides protection for a mundane doorway, gate or even portcullis from lesser elemental attacks and spells cast at it up to 3 points in strength.

description: Allows the caster to place their hands on the stones framing a mystical portal and see through it to the remote link. To maintain the vision the caster must close their eyes and remain touching the stone.. Mystical Glyphs All-powerful glyphs that are woven into, and form, the very fabric of the world. Most are usable as both subject and action. As subjects these symbols will often have slightly different meaning than that gained from their use as an action.
short_description: Allows the caster to place their hands on the stones framing a mystical portal and see through it to the remote link.

description: Increases both the weight and density of the enchanter (the equivalent of +4 Size), giving them a better chance (+20%) to resist being knocked-back in combat or being blown over by high winds or similar effects. The enchanter will also gain +1 Hit Point for the spell’s duration, (though damage taken whilst the spell is running will still be taken from the caster’s Hit Points after the spell has ended).
short_description: Increases both the weight and density of the enchanter (the equivalent of +4 Size), giving them a better chance (+20%) to resist being knocked-back in combat or being blown over by high winds or si...

description: Makes the caster appear paler and less substantial, their blood vessels showing more clearly through their skin. They will also become physically lighter, the equivalent of 4 SIZ points and will lose 2HP for the spell’s duration. They will resist knockback at -20%, and will be more vulnerable to being blown over by high winds or explosions. They will however be able to catch tantalising glimpses of the spirit world at the edges of vision.
short_description: Makes the caster appear paler and less substantial, their blood vessels showing more clearly through their skin.

description: Changes up to 1 cubic Lk of the substance touched to another random material. The material does not have to be a single block, a handful of coins in contact with each other can just as easily be changed Usually the material will remain as the same state e.g. a solid will remain a solid, a liquid a liquid, a gas a gas. Some elementals will always do the same thing, for example always turn water into liquor, lead in to gold etc. If the caster wants a specific result they may attempt to concentrate by making an INTx3 roll. On the whole the elementals will try to be accommodating, but ultimately the result is at the discretion of the referee.
short_description: Changes up to 1 cubic Lk of the substance touched to another random material.

description: Allows the caster to secrete quantities of a translucent silvery resinous material from their symbol hand. Known as ghanite, it is initially exuded as a thick liquid that can be shaped or moulded like plaster that sets stiff and brittle within 1R. The caster will produce roughly a skin-full each round. Once set, ghanite is similar to sugar-glass (with about 4HP and 4 Strength per 1LK section), although it can deteriorate over time and ancient ghanite might crumble to the touch. Waterproof but not gas-tight Ghanite can be used creatively in many ways and will stick to most surfaces, sealing passages, even holding back water (if layered thickly enough). Despite its weakness, ghanite can also be used as a sort of glue to quickly secure wood and other objects to create a temporary repair. Once the spell ends the ghanite produced will remain indefinitely.
short_description: Allows the caster to secrete quantities of a translucent silvery resinous material from their symbol hand.

description: Enables the caster to carve marks with their finger on the surface of a material. The caster may even leave marks on the surface of relatively still liquids. The marks can be in the form of writing, drawings or other symbols. They may make the marks whilst the spell is active. Once the spell ends the marks will remain on the surface of the material for up to a day before they fade.
short_description: Enables the caster to carve marks with their finger on the surface of a material. The caster may even leave marks on the surface of relatively still liquids.

description: Tells the caster roughly what the material is that they are touching. The elementals return a recognisable ‘feeling’ in response to different materials, and the caster will have been trained to recognise a great number of things when learning the spell, like pure gold, diamond, lacquered wood, fish skin etc. If the caster comes across a material they don’t know they may attempt a memorise skill roll if they encounter it again.
short_description: Tells the caster roughly what the material is that they are touching.

description: Causes the area of any material that the caster has touched to bear a form of contact poison. The area affected will have a radius of roughly 1Sd per level of the caster.Should a victim touch the affected area they will experience a sharp pain, like a sting. The sting will cause 1D8 damage to the victim, it will also cause a rapid stiffening of the finger joints reducing DFT by 1D4+4 points. Having been stung once by the affected material the victim will not suffer further damage if they contact it again whilst the spell is active. Any DFT lost will recover at a rate of 1 point each hour.
short_description: Causes the area of any material that the caster has touched to bear a form of contact poison.

description: Makes a material more resilient, effectively increasing its armour protection by +3 points. The material will also become more resistant to fire, melting, denting or tearing. The spell may be cast on normal armour but not alongside of any other armour spells. It may also be cast upon a living creature’s skin but it will slow it down by an equal amount of AG. An area of roughly 1Sd may be protected with this spell.
short_description: Makes a material more resilient, effectively increasing its armour protection by +3 points. The material will also become more resistant to fire, melting, denting or tearing.

description: Causes 1D4 points of damage to the first thing the caster touches after casting. If the caster touches a living creature they must overcome the creature’s KEY. The affected area is likely to appear crazed or stained as if aged by time.
short_description: Causes 1D4 points of damage to the first thing the caster touches after casting. If the caster touches a living creature they must overcome the creature’s KEY.

description: This spell must be used with a special white sealing wax which can be purchased from an alchemist for about 1G. The wax is melted and a seal placed upon a letter, door, box or whatever. Before the wax sets the spell should then be cast into it and a passphrase spoken. The spell will remain on the seal indefinitely and can only be safely broken if someone speaks the correct passphrase at the same time. If the seal is broken without the utterance of the passphrase the seal will explode causing 2D10 damage to anyone within 1Sd, and 1D10 to anyone within 2Sd. A document would be destroyed and lightweight wooden items will suffer damage, although a thick door will usually only suffer minor damage.
short_description: This spell must be used with a special white sealing wax which can be purchased from an alchemist for about 1G. The wax is melted and a seal placed upon a letter, door, box or whatever.

description: Makes a material more resilient, effectively increasing its armour protection by +6 points. The material will also become resistant to fire, melting, bending or tearing. The spell may be cast on normal armour but not alongside of any other armour spells. It may also be cast upon a living creature’s skin but it will slow it down by an equal amount of AG. An area of roughly 2Sd square may be protected with this spell. For example a stained glass window, a cart or a boat’s hull.
short_description: Makes a material more resilient, effectively increasing its armour protection by +6 points. The material will also become resistant to fire, melting, bending or tearing.

description: Hides the substance that the target is made from by making it appear to be a different material. It can make silk look like sack-cloth. Wood appear to be stone. Grass appear to be water. The volume affected depends on how broad the caster wishes the effect to be. The spell can affect a single tiny object or all of that type of material in a 20Sd range of the caster. If it is a forest and the caster wishes to affect the trees, then every tree in range will be affected. The eventual appearance usually varies according to the whim of the elementals, but if the caster concentrates by making an INTx3 roll they may determine how the affected material is changed.
short_description: Hides the substance that the target is made from by making it appear to be a different material. It can make silk look like sack-cloth.

description: Creates a lump of soft black waxy material in the palm of the caster’s hand. The wax may be used as a sealing wax on a document, box, doorway etc. The wax will harden over the course of 12R and whilst it is still setting the caster may cast a further spell into the seal, and if desired speak a password. Once set, should the seal be broken without the utterance of the passphrase, the spell within it will be activated taking its effect as if the caster had just cast it. As with Teshet stored spells the penalty for casting such spells is that the key points used will only regenerate at a rate of 1 Point each week. The Wax can be created and kept indefinitely before being used as a seal. The key used to make the wax will regenerate at the normal rate.
short_description: Creates a lump of soft black waxy material in the palm of the caster’s hand. The wax may be used as a sealing wax on a document, box, doorway etc.

description: Extrudes a thin film from the surface of the caster’s body and their accoutrements over the course of 1R. The film will detach as it grows, eventually forming a perfect inflated sphere about the caster, like a giant bubble. Within there will be sufficient air to last the spell. The bubble will protect the caster form gas, drowning, sinking or even just rain. The sphere will also save the caster from an impact from falling or being hit by a large object. Additionally the sphere is surprisingly resilient and will provide 1D4 points of armour protection against stabbing, cutting or impaling attacks. It is particularly effective protection against punching, and other bludgeoning weapons, offering almost complete protection. NB being spherical in nature it is possible for the caster to be rolled around if someone were to try pushing the sphere. Save from impact. Whilst bladed weapons will penetrate the surface of the bubble it will not burst, though it may sag somewhat.
short_description: Extrudes a thin film from the surface of the caster’s body and their accoutrements over the course of 1R.
