description: Adds a further 12R to the duration of a currently running spell. This will not work on spells that have a normal duration of less than 12R. The spell may only be used to lengthen a spell’s duration once. This may be used on spells that have been cast by another enchanter.
short_description: Adds a further 12R to the duration of a currently running spell. This will not work on spells that have a normal duration of less than 12R.

description: Cancels an existing 1 point spell (ie those cast with just Evu or Sen as the action). This combination will not effect permanent spells.
short_description: Cancels an existing 1 point spell (ie those cast with just Evu or Sen as the action). This combination will not effect permanent spells.

description: Doubles the duration of another spell. Thus most 12R spells would last 24R, 1R spells will last 2R. V 1 and V 2 spells will have doubled duration depending on the level of the caster. The caster may double spells cast by another enchanter. The spell must be cast on a spell that the Enchanter has cast themselves. It is best cast immediately after the original spell is cast as the doubling effect will only double the time the spell has left.
short_description: Doubles the duration of another spell. Thus most 12R spells would last 24R, 1R spells will last 2R.

description: Cancels an existing 1 or 2 glyph spell. Should this be used against a 1 or 2 glyph permanent spell, it will negate the effects for 12R. Casting this spell with the Permanence symbol would enable the caster to dispel a 1 glyph permanent spell (the caster sacrificing a point of their own permanent KEY to dispel the point of permanent KEY used in the target spell).
short_description: Cancels an existing 1 or 2 glyph spell. Should this be used against a 1 or 2 glyph permanent spell, it will negate the effects for 12R.

description: Informs the enchanter of any other spells operating within range. The combination may also give a vague idea of what kind of magic. For example: an earth glyph spell, or even a sorcerous spell and whether it is dangerous or benign..
short_description: Informs the enchanter of any other spells operating within range. The combination may also give a vague idea of what kind of magic.

description: Allows the caster to transfer a spell they have cast upon themselves to a willing recipient. This will only work with spells that are currently running. The transferred spell will still only run for the rounds remaining from its original casting.
short_description: Allows the caster to transfer a spell they have cast upon themselves to a willing recipient. This will only work with spells that are currently running.

description: If the caster successfully resists an offensive spell that has been cast at them, then the Key that was used to fuel that spell will be stripped out and added to the caster’s own. This spell will be used up in the process. This Key cannot be used to increase the caster’s KEY above maximum, but it may be used to top up a piece of Arbine.
short_description: If the caster successfully resists an offensive spell that has been cast at them, then the Key that was used to fuel that spell will be stripped out and added to the caster’s own.

description: Allows the caster to exchange as many points of Key as needed with a single willing recipient. This spell cannot be used to raise the effective KEY of the caster or recipient above their normal maximum. This cannot be used with Key already stored in Arbine, but may be used to top up a piece..
short_description: Allows the caster to exchange as many points of Key as needed with a single willing recipient.

description: Enables the caster to overcome the KEY of a target at the caster’s normal full KEY+1 per level in enchanting when casting spells at them. Thus a level 3 enchanter with a KEY of 21 may cast a spell and attempt to overcome the KEY of the target with an effective KEY of 24. This extra KEY belongs to the elemental spirits the caster cannot use it to make resistance rolls against spells cast at them or to fuel spells.
short_description: Enables the caster to overcome the KEY of a target at the caster’s normal full KEY+1 per level in enchanting when casting spells at them.

description: Enables the caster to resist spells, or similar at their normal full KEY+1 per level in enchanting. This KEY belongs to the elemental spirit and cannot be used to overcome a target’s KEY when casting spells or to fuel any.
short_description: Enables the caster to resist spells, or similar at their normal full KEY+1 per level in enchanting.

description: Conceals the magical aura given off by the caster, as well as any spells or magical objects which they have upon them. The spell will effectively hide them from most magical detection spells or scrying. The spell will also protect the caster from setting off any XAMA CYA spells, as these rely on a victim’s aura to be triggered.
short_description: Conceals the magical aura given off by the caster, as well as any spells or magical objects which they have upon them.

description: Allows the caster to attempt to steal 6 points of KEY from the target, if they fail to resist. The stolen key goes to top up the caster’s own KEY if it is depleted, including magical storage devices. The caster cannot reduce a target’s KEY to less than one. If they were to attempt this the spell would still be cast but225 they would not receive any KEY in return. This also applies if the target’s KEY is at 6 or less when the spell is cast.
short_description: Allows the caster to attempt to steal 6 points of KEY from the target, if they fail to resist. The stolen key goes to top up the caster’s own KEY if it is depleted, including magical storage devices.

description: If the caster successfully resists an offensive spell that has been cast at them, then the Key that was used to fuel that spell will be stripped out and added to the caster’s own. This spell will be cancelled by 4 point spells or higher, but not 1,2 or 3 point combinations.. This key cannot be used to increase the caster’s KEY above maximum, but it may be used to top up a piece of Arbine.
short_description: If the caster successfully resists an offensive spell that has been cast at them, then the Key that was used to fuel that spell will be stripped out and added to the caster’s own.

description: Wards the caster from magical attacks and spells up to 3 glyphs in length. 1 and 2 glyph spells will be repelled without affecting this spell. 3 glyph spells or the attacks of lesser elementals will only be warded for 1R before this spell fails.
short_description: Wards the caster from magical attacks and spells up to 3 glyphs in length. 1 and 2 glyph spells will be repelled without affecting this spell.

description: Wraps the caster in an almost transparent cloak of silver. The combination enables the caster to resist spells or similar magical attacks at their normal full KEY+2 per level, regardless of what their current KEY is. This KEY is provided by the elementals and cannot be used to fuel spells or to overcome another’s KEY when casting a spell.
short_description: Wraps the caster in an almost transparent cloak of silver.

description: Cancels an existing 1, 2, or 3 point spell.. It will have no effect on 4 glyph combinations. If cast upon a 1, or 2 point permanent spell, it will negate the effects for 12R. A 2 glyph permanent spell may be dispelled using the Permanence symbol ∞ (the caster sacrificing 2 points of their own permanent KEY to dispel the points of permanent KEY used in the original spell). This is the most powerful spell to generically cancel other spells. The only way cancel anything more powerful is to cast the same spell combination as that to be cancelled and add the CHOM glyph to the end.
short_description: Cancels an existing 1, 2, or 3 point spell.. It will have no effect on 4 glyph combinations.

description: Prevents another enchanter from seeing the caster using magical means. Thus the caster might pass before a magical eye (EJA CYA) and not be seen. The spell will also prevent them from being observed using a scrying spell of some sort
short_description: Prevents another enchanter from seeing the caster using magical means. Thus the caster might pass before a magical eye (EJA CYA) and not be seen.

description: Warns the caster if they are being scried by magic of some form. The spell will not, however, tell who is attempting to scry the caster out, nor how far away.
short_description: Warns the caster if they are being scried by magic of some form. The spell will not, however, tell who is attempting to scry the caster out, nor how far away.

description: Enables the caster to detect if an object is magical simply by touching it. It will also give the caster an idea if the object works by enchanting, sorcery or some other magic, as well as some hint of function (such as: offensive, defensive, powerful, intelligent). It will not, though, tell the caster how the object works. Several objects may be touched within the two minute duration of the spell.
short_description: Enables the caster to detect if an object is magical simply by touching it.

description: Causes a willing recipient to become a sentinel. As a result their skin and eyes taking on a pale silvery glow, but they will otherwise be unaffected by the spell. The magic allows the sentinel to ward a 20Sd radius area about them from any magical attacks up to 4 glyphs in length. 1, 2 or 3 glyph spells will be dispelled as soon as they enter the sentinel’s range. The sentinel may ward against a 4 glyph spell for 1R before the sentinel spell itself fails. Spells of 5 glyphs or more and direct attacks from elemental drakes will break this spell. If the sentinel is killed then the spell will cease to function.
short_description: Causes a willing recipient to become a sentinel. As a result their skin and eyes taking on a pale silvery glow, but they will otherwise be unaffected by the spell.

description: Causes the target object to become a sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd about it from any magical attacks up to 3 glyphs in length. It will also ward against any 4 glyph spells for 1R before failing. Spells of 5 glyphs or more and direct attacks from elemental drakes will break this spell. If the sentinel object is destroyed then the spell will cease to function. The sentinel object will appear to give off a pale silvery glow.
short_description: Causes the target object to become a sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd about it from any magical attacks up to 3 glyphs in length. It will also ward against any 4 glyph spells for 1R before failing.

description: Causes a willing recipient to become a greater sentinel. As a result their skin and eyes will take on a distinct silvery glow and they will seem to generate pale silvery wisps as they move. The sentinel will otherwise seem unaffected by the spell whilst it is running. The magic allows the sentinel to ward a 20Sd radius area about them from any magical attacks up to 5 glyphs in length.. 1, 2, 3 or 4 glyph spells will be dispelled as soon as they enter the sentinel’s range. The sentinel will ward against a 5 glyph spell for 1R before the sentinel spell itself fails. Spells of 6 glyphs or more will break this spell. If the sentinel is killed then the spell will cease to function. When the spell ends the recipient will lose 1D10 points of Key (recoverable at the normal rate).
short_description: Causes a willing recipient to become a greater sentinel. As a result their skin and eyes will take on a distinct silvery glow and they will seem to generate pale silvery wisps as they move.

description: Causes the target object to become a greater sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd about it from any magical attacks up to 5 glyphs in length. 1, 2, 3 and 4 glyph spells will be dispelled as soon as they enter the sentinel object’s range. The sentinel will ward against a 5 glyph spell for 1R before the sentinel spell itself fails. Spells of 6 glyphs or more will break this spell. If the sentinel object is destroyed then the spell will cease to function. The sentinel object will appear to give off a pale silvery glow.
short_description: Causes the target object to become a greater sentinel, warding an area of 20Sd about it from any magical attacks up to 5 glyphs in length.

description: Causes a willing recipient to become a high sentinel, warding an area of 40Sd about them from any magical attacks by elemental drakes or spells up to 5 glyph in length. It will also protect from the unlikely event of a 6 point spell for 1R before failing. If the sentinel is killed then the spell will cease to function. Once the spell is cast the sentinel will double in apparent size, bursting from any clothing or armour. Their eyes will glow bright silver, their skin will change to resemble an unknown night sky of black and silver, emitting a beautiful yet disturbing magical aura. At the end of the spell the recipient’s body will almost certainly craze and crumble to dust. It is not believed that anyone has yet survived being a sentinel of this level.
short_description: Causes a willing recipient to become a high sentinel, warding an area of 40Sd about them from any magical attacks by elemental drakes or spells up to 5 glyph in length.
Spell Glyphs: Maef + Ghan + Nanu + Gis + Cya + Evu

description: A large object is required to funnel the power of being a high sentinel, warding an area of 40Sd about it from any magical attacks by elemental drakes or spells up to 5 glyphs in length. It will also protect from the unlikely event of a 6 point spell for 1R before failing. Once cast the surface of the sentinel object will change to resemble an unknown night sky of black and silver, emitting a beautiful yet disturbing magical aura. If the sentinel object is destroyed then the spell will cease to function, Often at the end of the spell the object will craze and crumble apart, but some well made statues carved from the hardest and densest stone have proved resistant..
short_description: A large object is required to funnel the power of being a high sentinel, warding an area of 40Sd about it from any magical attacks by elemental drakes or spells up to 5 glyphs in length.
Spell Glyphs: Maef + Ghan + Teshet + Gis + Cya + Evu

description: Halves the normal healing time on a single wound, or damaged attribute. Thus a wound will recover 2 Hit Points per day, a broken bone will knit in half the time and VRG will regenerate at 2 points per day. The spell will not heal something which would not have healed by itself. Thus an organ damaged beyond repair, or a severed limb will not be restored, although the recipient’s Hit Points may be healed.
short_description: Halves the normal healing time on a single wound, or damaged attribute.

description: Causes all wounds or damaged attributes the target has suffered to take twice as long to heal as they normally might. Thus a wound will only recover 1 Hit Point every two days, and an attribute such as AG will only be regained at 1 point every two days. If magical healing is cast upon the wound it will require twice as many spell points for each Hit Point healed. The caster must overcome the target’s Key for the spell to work.
short_description: Causes all wounds or damaged attributes the target has suffered to take twice as long to heal as they normally might.

description: A fertility spell often requested by couples trying to have a child. It is also used by farmers who are hoping to breed their livestock successfully.
short_description: A fertility spell often requested by couples trying to have a child. It is also used by farmers who are hoping to breed their livestock successfully.

description: Stops a living plant, creature or person from growing, multiplying or conceiving for the duration of the spell. It will be necessary for the caster to overcome the Key of a living creature or person if they are not a willing recipient. If cast upon an infected wound it will stop the infection from getting any worse. However it will also prevent the wound from healing (or even stopping bleeding) for the duration of the spell. This may also be used on an individual affected by a disease in the same way. Again it will prevent the regeneration of lost attribute points whilst pausing the progress of the illness. Once the spell has finished the process will continue as normal.
short_description: Stops a living plant, creature or person from growing, multiplying or conceiving for the duration of the spell.

description: Allows the caster to detect the presence of any living creatures of SIZ 1, or larger within range. The caster will receive a vague idea of distance and direction, but will only gain a rough idea of numbers unless they are well dispersed.
short_description: Allows the caster to detect the presence of any living creatures of SIZ 1, or larger within range.

description: Compels many small life-forms from the surrounding area to gather at the caster’s feet. The life-forms will generally be less than SIZ 1 and can include worms, bugs, small rodents, even birds. Sometimes larger creatures are also drawn by the spell. Such creatures can be handled by the caster whilst the spell is running.
short_description: Compels many small life-forms from the surrounding area to gather at the caster’s feet. The life-forms will generally be less than SIZ 1 and can include worms, bugs, small rodents, even birds.

description: Enables the caster to donate 4 of their own Hit Points to a wounded recipient. The Hit Points will help heal all the recipient’s wounds. Once a recipient has received healing in this way, they will not be able to have additional healing on specific wounds. The caster, meanwhile, will suffer a form of bruising damage. Hit Points above the recipient’s normal full amount will leech away at a rate of 1 point every minute. Hit Points that the caster has lost in this way will heal at the normal rate. Using Key from Arbine for this spell will double the amount that can be transferred, to 8 HP.
short_description: Enables the caster to donate 4 of their own Hit Points to a wounded recipient. The Hit Points will help heal all the recipient’s wounds.

description: Enables the caster to steal 1D4 Hit Points from the target. The caster must overcome the target’s KEY to succeed. The stolen HP will help to heal any generic wounds that the caster has. Wounds healed in this way cannot have any further magical healing applied to them The caster may still steal HP to make their own go above the normal full amount, but such HP will leech away at a rate of a point each round. Using Key from Arbine to cast this spell will double the amount of HP that may be stolen to 2D4 HP.
short_description: Enables the caster to steal 1D4 Hit Points from the target. The caster must overcome the target’s KEY to succeed.

description: Causes the blade or head of the affected weapon to begin teeming with life: fungus, mould, bugs, bacteria its all there. If the weapon were to hit a target even if no damage was done some of this ‘life’ would begin growing on the target. If the weapon were to actually cause a wound the wound would start to grow mushrooms, bugs and other tiny living things too grim to mention. The effect would cause no extra damage except some severe psychological trauma. Once the spell ends the life will simply crumble to dust and vanish. Any wounds caused by the weapon remain as normal wounds. Such a weapon will not have any additional effect on undead creatures.
short_description: Causes the blade or head of the affected weapon to begin teeming with life: fungus, mould, bugs, bacteria its all there.

description: Protects the caster from any infections or diseases which they come into contact with for the duration of the spell. The combination will not cure an existing infection or disease that the caster already has.
short_description: Protects the caster from any infections or diseases which they come into contact with for the duration of the spell.

description: Enables the caster to tell, simply by looking, if a person or creature is alive or not. The spell will also tell them if the spirit of a dead person or creature has departed the body or not.
short_description: Enables the caster to tell, simply by looking, if a person or creature is alive or not. The spell will also tell them if the spirit of a dead person or creature has departed the body or not.

description: If the caster overcomes their KEY, this spell causes the target to hear the noises of every living thing about them getting on with living, right down to the tiniest microbe. Unless it is in a practically sterile area the mass of sound will drive the target to distraction, reducing their chance to carry out any action, including attacking and parrying, to half chance.
short_description: If the caster overcomes their KEY, this spell causes the target to hear the noises of every living thing about them getting on with living, right down to the tiniest microbe.

description: Cast on a bladed or pointed weapon this spell will not appear to change the affected weapon in any way. However should the caster choose to lay the weapon back upon any wounds caused by the weapon for 1R such a wound may be healed by up to 4HP. This will include the recovery of any attributes or blood lost. The spell will also heal fatal wounds caused by the weapon, (within a reasonable timeframe) bringing the victim back to life in the process. The spell will only work whilst it is still running, the healing effect will not work on wounds caused prior to the spell being cast. Any wounds not healed before the spell ends cannot be healed in this way. If parts of the victim were severed, they must be put loosely in place for the healing effect to be successful. The usual use for this spell is by practitioners of the healing arts undertaking surgery.
short_description: Cast on a bladed or pointed weapon this spell will not appear to change the affected weapon in any way.

description: Allows the caster to shoot a long filament of life force from each hand, once each round, up to 2Sd range . The filaments take the form of a light soft white material, not unlike fungus or wool. If a tendril hits a target it will attach itself and begin to grow across the target’s body, branching and growing into two more filaments at the start of each round. Whilst the filaments are inherently weak and may easily be pulled off, they multiply quickly and begin to restrict movement. The target may attempt to remove a filament using a basic attack or DFTx3 roll. As with any attack they may make a second attack if they have not had to parry or dodge in that round (and if they have a current AG and DFT of 12 or above) For every two filaments attached to the target’s body they will lose a point of AG and DFT and if they fail to pull enough off they will quickly become totally restrained in a huge ball of growing filaments. It should be noted that if someone else should come into contact with someone covered by the filaments they risk a 50% chance of each round of being struck with 1D2 filaments. At the end of the spell the filaments will begin to turn dry and grey, crumbling away from the target over the course of 1D6 Rounds.
short_description: Allows the caster to shoot a long filament of life force from each hand, once each round, up to 2Sd range . The filaments take the form of a light soft white material, not unlike fungus or wool.

description: Requires a seed, egg, chrysalis or any small living thing which has a high life ‘potential’ but is not likely to crawl off or die for lack of water or food. Once set upon the seed the spell will remain dormant until someone comes within 1Sd range; whereupon the seed will split open and grow some legs or tendrils. The new creature, though no larger than a 2-3 Rg across is a ravenous eating machine and will attack anything which is moving within its field of view. The creature is able to jump up to 2Sd in the air and can crawl up any but the smoothest surfaces. Whilst it only does 1 point of damage for each attack it is quite capable of eating its way through leather armour (at 1 point per round) or finding its way through gaps in plated armour. Such a creature is easily squashed but on the following round 1D4 more creatures will grow from the remains. Each creature will also split into a further 1D4 every 4 rounds anyway. After 12R all the creatures will have lived their full life-span and will promptly die Each creature will have the following characteristics. Note they will not have KEY as such, because the Key that keeps it alive comes from the spell (dispel the magic and the creatures will die). Incineration, freezing, and acid will also stop them from multiplying. STR 1 HP 1 INT 1 SIZ 1 weapon attack damage STR 1 bite 50% 1 VGR 1 AG 12 skills: dodge 20%, jump 70%
short_description: Requires a seed, egg, chrysalis or any small living thing which has a high life ‘potential’ but is not likely to crawl off or die for lack of water or food.

description: This spell will not only protect the caster from any diseases and infections for the duration of the spell but will also act to ward away any small living creatures such as insects, rats, snakes etc.. The spell may put off large curious creatures such as bears but will not deter anything which is hungry or determined.
short_description: This spell will not only protect the caster from any diseases and infections for the duration of the spell but will also act to ward away any small living creatures such as insects, rats, snakes etc..

description: Enables the caster to steal some of the ‘life force’ from the target’s body. The caster must overcome the KEY of a victim to be successful. This has the effect of ‘necrotising’ the victims flesh, in that they will seem more cadaverous. It will also mean that the target loses 1D4+1 points of Key to the caster in the form of pure life energy, which the caster can use to heal a damaged attribute or even temporarily add to an undamaged one. This can include HP, STR, VGR, AG or DFT. If the caster uses the points to increase an attribute beyond its maximum, the extra points will leech away at a rate of 1 per hour. For every 2 Key points the victim loses in life energy they will also lose 1 point from each of the attributes listed. The victim will heal their lost life force at a rate of 1 point per day. The spell will not reduce any of the victim’s attributes below 1, although once their Key is reduced to 1 their body will be completely necrotised and they will be barely recognisable as a living creature. Note that if the caster casts this as a permanent spell, sacrificing three points of their attribute KEY in the process, the caster’s attribute(s) will be permanently raised, whilst the victim’s flesh will remain necrotised permanently. Worse, should the caster cast this spell normally several times until the victim is nearly on 1 point of Key, then cast this spell permanently, reducing their KEY to 1, the victim will forever be fully Necrotised unable to regenerate their own KEY or attributes. This spell is clearly a very dangerous. Its teaching is forbidden by the Tree of Life religions and its knowledge actively suppressed. With the barrier in place most people affected by this spell will regenerate their damaged KEY and other attributes naturally over time. However should the barrier to the Greylands be breached, or the game action take place in a different world space where undead are more commonplace, then the GM may wish to allow Necris (those that have been fully necrotised) to become a thing in their own right. Necris will have the ability to make a touch attack to recover lost life energy in the same fashion as this spell. This energy will only last a few hours, so one Necri may be responsible for the generation of several more before they have fully healed.
short_description: Enables the caster to steal some of the ‘life force’ from the target’s body. The caster must overcome the KEY of a victim to be successful.

description: This spell has the same effects as Ix-GhanEkat, in that it enables the caster to steal some of the ‘life force’ from the target’s body. It enables the caster to steal away 1D8+2 points of Key in the form of pure life energy, which the caster can use to heal a damaged attribute or even temporarily add to an undamaged one. This can include HP, STR, VGR, AG or DFT. If the caster uses the points to increase an attribute beyond its maximum, the extra points will leech away at a rate of 1 per hour. This has the effect of ‘necrotising’ the victims flesh, in that they will seem more cadaverous. For every 2 Key points the victim loses in life energy they will also lose 1 point from each of the attributes listed. The victim will heal their lost life force at a rate of 1 point per day. The spell will not reduce any of the victim’s attributes below 1, although once their Key is reduced to 1 their body will be completely necrotised and they will be barely recognisable as a living creature. Note that if the caster casts this as a permanent spell, sacrificing three points of their attribute KEY in the process, the caster’s attribute(s) will be permanently raised, whilst the victim’s flesh will remain necrotised permanently. Worse, should the caster cast this spell normally several times until the victim is nearly on 1 point of Key, then cast this spell permanently, reducing their KEY to 1, the victim will forever be fully Necrotised unable to regenerate their own KEY or attributes.
short_description: This spell has the same effects as Ix-GhanEkat, in that it enables the caster to steal some of the ‘life force’ from the target’s body.

description: Cast upon an individual at the point of death this spell will immediately send their soul to the afterlife. This spell will not dispatch someone simply on 0 HP. They must be on negative HP and in the process of dying already.
short_description: Cast upon an individual at the point of death this spell will immediately send their soul to the afterlife. This spell will not dispatch someone simply on 0 HP.

description: Cast upon an individual at, or shortly after, the point of death, this spell will halt the process of the soul leaving the body for the duration of the spell. This may allow time for magical healing or other magics to be cast upon the individual. Once the spell ends, if the individual has not been magically brought back to life, the soul will continue to leave the body and depart as normal. It should be noted that this spell cannot bring the body back to life.
short_description: Cast upon an individual at, or shortly after, the point of death, this spell will halt the process of the soul leaving the body for the duration of the spell.

description: Much like Ylg-Sen, this spell will halt the process of the soul leaving the body. The spell will not bring the deceased back to life, but other than becoming cold their body will not start to decay or stiffen whilst the spell endures and magical healing or other magics may be attempted during this time. Once the spell ends, if the individual has not been magically brought back to life, the soul will continue to leave the body and depart as normal.
short_description: Much like Ylg-Sen, this spell will halt the process of the soul leaving the body.

description: Summons a disembodied spirit and makes it visible to the naked eye. The spell will work with a ghost or guardian spirit that is somehow tied to a specific place or object. The spell also be used to summon a once living spirit which is not ‘at rest’ or only very recently (within 24 hours) dead. The spirit will be unable to talk but may chose to answer questions by nodding or shaking its head. After the spell is ended the spirit will vanish.
short_description: Summons a disembodied spirit and makes it visible to the naked eye. The spell will work with a ghost or guardian spirit that is somehow tied to a specific place or object.

description: Enables the enchanter to detect any disembodied spirits or ‘undead’ creatures within the range. The spell will give a vague idea of distance and direction as well as some idea of how powerful the detected entity is.
short_description: Enables the enchanter to detect any disembodied spirits or ‘undead’ creatures within the range.

description: Causes blade or head of the affected weapon to noticeably darken. Whilst it will cause no extra damage to a living person, a disembodied spirit will seek to avoid its touch. In combat the weapon will cause +1 damage per level of the caster to ‘physical’ undead creatures such as Oni or revenants. It will also allow the weapon to case half weapon damage to normally invulnerable undead creatures such as vampires and wraiths.
short_description: Causes blade or head of the affected weapon to noticeably darken. Whilst it will cause no extra damage to a living person, a disembodied spirit will seek to avoid its touch.

description: Acts as a simple ward against both discorporate (once living) spirits and also undead creatures. Should a discorporate spirit or undead creature come within 1Sd of the caster it must resist against the caster’s KEY. If it fails to resist, the power of the YLG symbol will keep it at bay. Even it does resist, it must continue making the resistance rolls each round it spends within range until the spell ends. The spell will not ward against elemental spirits.
short_description: Acts as a simple ward against both discorporate (once living) spirits and also undead creatures.

description: Enables the caster to see the true form of any discorporate entities that are on, or close to, the boundary of the spirit world. It will show any spirits which would normally be invisible. This includes ghosts, ineral spirits, even wraiths. The spell only makes it possible to observe these spirits, not to communicate with them. The caster will only be aware of elemental sprits as a seemingly orchestrated movement permeating everything, as their true form is not something the human mind can easily comprehend.
short_description: Enables the caster to see the true form of any discorporate entities that are on, or close to, the boundary of the spirit world. It will show any spirits which would normally be invisible.

description: Enables the caster to hear disembodied spirits for the duration of the spell. The sprits will not be able to hear the caster. It should be noted that the caster will not necessarily even understand what the spirits are saying, Many speak in different languages, some simply utter nonsense.
short_description: Enables the caster to hear disembodied spirits for the duration of the spell. The sprits will not be able to hear the caster.

description: Causes the blade or head of the affected weapon to emit an eldritch glow. The weapon will cause +2 damage per level of the caster to ‘physical’ undead creatures such as Oni or revenants. It will also do normal weapon damage to invulnerable undead creatures such as Vampires and wraiths. Any disembodied spirits will try to avoid the weapon’s touch. If the weapon is used to strike a disembodied spirit, the spirit will suffer 1D4 Key loss. Whilst the affected weapon will cause no extra damage to a living creature, it will make them experience the ‘little death’ effect each time they are struck. This is the feeling experienced of having your Spirit momentarily shaken loose of your physical body .
short_description: Causes the blade or head of the affected weapon to emit an eldritch glow. The weapon will cause +2 damage per level of the caster to ‘physical’ undead creatures such as Oni or revenants.

description: This spell requires a dead creature, a beetle or a mouse is quite sufficient. The kind is unimportant it just has to be dead. Once set upon the creature the spell will remain dormant until someone, or something, comes within 1Sd. Should it be triggered by a living creature they must attempt to resist the caster’s KEY (at the time of casting even if this was a long time ago). If they resist they will merely experience the ‘little death’ effect of having their spirit shaken loose in their body. If they fail, their spirit will actually be shaken from their body and become temporarily suspended beyond the boundary for 2D6 Rounds. Their soul will still be connected tenuously to their body, but to all but a master healer they will appear dead. until their spirit returns. If the weapon is triggered by a vampire or revenant or other undead creature, the object will explode causing the creature 3D6 points of damage.
short_description: This spell requires a dead creature, a beetle or a mouse is quite sufficient. The kind is unimportant it just has to be dead.

description: Causes two dark, ghostly lashes to extend from the caster’s hands up to 1Sd long. The lashes will be divided into several thinner and more ragged strips towards the ends. The caster may use the lashes much like a whip to strike at a target. The caster will hit at basic attack, unless they have a whip skill. If it hits any discorporate being, an undead being such as an oni, vampire or wraith, or a manifest spirit such as an elemental the lash will strip 1D6 points of Key from the being. As they are ghostly in nature, the lashes are not possible to parry, nor will armour protect from them–unless the parrying weapon or armour is magically enabled to do so (or it is made of blood silver). Whilst the lash will cause no damage to a living creature, it will make them experience the ‘little death’ effect each time they are struck. This is the feeling experienced of having your Spirit momentarily shaken loose of your physical body .
short_description: Causes two dark, ghostly lashes to extend from the caster’s hands up to 1Sd long. The lashes will be divided into several thinner and more ragged strips towards the ends.

description: Acts as a ward against both discorporate (once living) spirits and also undead creatures. The caster must draw the glyphs on whatever is to be warded. This can include themselves, another living creature, or even a portable object. Should a discorporate spirit or undead creature attempt to enter the field of protection it must resist against the caster’s KEY. If it fails to resist it will be unable to come any closer, repelled by the YLG glyph. If the entity does resist it will have to repeat the resistance roll each round until the spell ends. The spell is not effcetive against elemental spirits.
short_description: Acts as a ward against both discorporate (once living) spirits and also undead creatures. The caster must draw the glyphs on whatever is to be warded.

description: Causes the caster to appear dead for the duration of the spell. For the spell to take effect the caster must be reasonably relaxed. Once it has taken effect the caster will seem to stop breathing and their heartbeat will be undetectable. Their eyes will remain fixed, the skin turn pale and even a stiffening similar to rigor mortis will set into the caster’s body. If examined by a healer they will be pronounced dead. Only magic will reveal that some life remains within the body. Once the spell has ended the caster will come back to life and full consciousness over the course of 12R.
short_description: Causes the caster to appear dead for the duration of the spell. For the spell to take effect the caster must be reasonably relaxed.

description: Enables the caster to speak to and understand disembodied spirits for the duration of the spell. There is no guarantee that such spirits will have anything to say to the caster or will speak more than gobbledygook. Using this spell the caster may also add +10% to their skill in Enchanting, since an elemental spirit will also be able to understand the caster better.
short_description: Enables the caster to speak to and understand disembodied spirits for the duration of the spell.

description: Sets an elemental spirit within the perimeter of a specially drawn circle as a guardian. Guardian Spirit INT 1D6+14 KEY 3D6+14. Any disembodied or manifest spirits which attempt to enter or leave the circle must make a KEY vs. KEY struggle with the elemental spirit to do so. A spirit entering will probably only try once. One which wishes to leave may try every six rounds. Such a circle may also be used to confine or repel wraiths, demons as well as undead creatures such as revenants and vampires. The circle may be drawn using most materials onto any mostly flat surface, as long as it represents a complete circle with a slightly smaller circle within the first. It will take at least 6R to draw as the caster must place glyphs for the spell at cardinal points The spell will remain within the circle for the duration of the spell or until it is broken either by a line being smudged or a living person crossing the perimeter.
short_description: Sets an elemental spirit within the perimeter of a specially drawn circle as a guardian. Guardian Spirit INT 1D6+14 KEY 3D6+14.

description: Cast upon a corpse this rare spell will make it utter simple yes or no answers to any questions. The answers will be given in the form of a sigh as air escapes the corpse’s throat, the corpse does not move its mouth or tongue and is not re-animated as a result of this spell. The corpse can only give answers about things it may have known whilst alive, and generally will answer truthfully. If it does not know or can’t remember the answer to a question it simply will not answer. The older the corpse the less likely it will be able to answer any given question as follows: up to 6 hours 100% 12 hours 90% 1 day 80% 2 days 70% 4 days 60% 1 week 50% 2 weeks 40% 1 month 30% 2 months 20% 4 months 10% 8 months 0% Within reason, if a corpse is particularly badly decayed, eaten, dissolved or otherwise damaged, it will probably not be able give any any answers.
short_description: Cast upon a corpse this rare spell will make it utter simple yes or no answers to any questions.

description: In the same manner as YLG-MEAF-Gis this spell will set an elemental spirit within the perimeter of a specially drawn circle as a guardian. The elemental spirit involved will be of a very powerful nature and have a KEY of 2D6+24. Any disembodied or manifest spirits which attempt to enter or leave the circle must make a KEY vs. KEY struggle with the elemental spirit to do so. A spirit entering will probably only try once. One which wishes to leave may try every six rounds. Such a circle may also be used to confine or repel wraiths, demons as well as undead creatures such as revenants and vampires. The circle may be drawn using most materials onto any mostly flat surface, as long as it represents a complete circle with a slightly smaller circle within the first. It will take at least 6R to draw as the caster must place glyphs for the spell at cardinal points The spell will remain within the circle for the duration of the spell or until it is broken either by a line being smudged or a living person crossing the perimeter.
